# English coach and working mom Monica Lim's digital planner keeps all the details straight 

English coach and working mom Monica Lim's digital planner keeps all the details straight
=========================================================================================

Monica Lim is an English consultant for high-powered people, from PR professionals to directors of top Korean beauty companies. With dozens of clients, she needs to keep their lesson plans and reading material organized and accessible. On top of that, she's a mom of two. Both her consulting business and family's schedule live in Notion so she can keep track of all the moving pieces.
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Insights from

![](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2FrYYRWHCyO9E98uHFLsoKE%2F85dba31096870315cd62fbe60c5fdacf%2FMonica_Lim.jpeg&w=96&q=80)

Monica Lim

Creator

Use cases

Personal Organization, Personal Notes

1

A planner that makes the big picture clear
------------------------------------------

Monica's daily calendar is so jam-packed with meetings that she almost has to schedule time to grab a snack. She needed one place to plan all her coaching sessions, while also having the flexibility to change those plans based on her clients' schedules and learning needs. For this, she uses a table in Notion, sorted by date and time, with links to the content she's planning to teach. Filters let her plan and view information by day, week, or month. Even with so many clients to juggle, Monica can make sure she's fully prepped for each meeting.

![Calendar](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F3nTrtSnf69ZkXvxHRfNK7I%2Ff7840b398618510b6f5d566f5914a7bc%2Fcalendar.png&w=1080&q=75)

Monica can link any of her databases so that she can keep all relevant information bundled.

### A library of all the best teaching resources

To help her clients work on their reading and comprehension skills, Monica collects interesting articles in a database. When she plans her week, it's easy to search this list and assign the articles most relevant for each client. She also tracks the number of times she's used an article to see what's working. No need to keep it all in her head. Notion eliminates the duplicative work so Monica can focus on being an effective teacher.

![](https://www.notion.com/front-static/pages/case-studies/quote-left-rectangle.png)

When I used Google Drive, I duplicated work because information took so long to find. In Notion, I can easily visualize my entire workspace. My clients love that all their resources are organized.

![Monica Lim](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2FrYYRWHCyO9E98uHFLsoKE%2F85dba31096870315cd62fbe60c5fdacf%2FMonica_Lim.jpeg&w=2048&q=75)

Monica Lim  
Creator

2

A virtual classroom for personalized learning
---------------------------------------------

Monica's client list is diverse, so she needs to personalize each experience based on their specific needs, skill level, and interest. Each client has a Notion page that acts as their home base. They can view notes from past sessions, see assigned readings and explore resources that Monica adds to the page.

![coffee cups](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F4qnI6qtSTGGiaRDVfMuJEt%2F4d9d69a38a063579754753075eeda3fa%2Fcoffee-cups.png&w=1080&q=75)

Multiple people can write in the same Notion page and see real-time edits.

### Instant feedback loop between teacher and student

Each coaching session has its own Notion page, where Monica can embed a link to the assigned reading and post discussion questions. As the client practices writing and speaking in English, Monica can make real-time corrections in the page and answer any questions about her edits. Since all lessons are saved in Notion pages, students can go back and reference them anytime to cement what they've learned.

![](https://www.notion.com/front-static/pages/case-studies/quote-right-oval.png)

Teaching requires a lot of back and forth with the student. Not only is Notion easy for collaboration but it's also fun to use.

![Monica Lim](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2FrYYRWHCyO9E98uHFLsoKE%2F85dba31096870315cd62fbe60c5fdacf%2FMonica_Lim.jpeg&w=2048&q=75)

Monica Lim  
Creator

3

A family hub to manage everyone's commitments
---------------------------------------------

In between lessons, Monica's organizing her kids' activities. Kindergarten, soccer, piano, ballet — it's easy to lose track of where they're supposed to be and when. In Notion, she manages both her kids' schedules in one database. All recurring activities can be viewed as a list, or she can switch to a board view to see the whole week. Peace of mind is hard to come by for this working mom, but keeping everything in Notion helps her stop worrying.

![Lighthouse ](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F5t0X9gE2uE6ZbsiYw8VuQu%2Fb44e3fdbbfe4245c708ea519eb1f331f%2Flighthouse.png&w=1080&q=75)

Monica can organize by any database property, including numbers, dates, or people.

### Never lose track of phone numbers or photos

For each activity, Monica can store all the important information in the same place. She no longer wastes time searching for a phone number, wondering whether she missed a payment, or finding an address. When the piano teacher sends photos or videos of her kids playing music, she can drop them in the page and keep those memories safe and simple to find.

![](https://www.notion.com/front-static/pages/case-studies/quote-left-blob.png)

Time with my kids is so precious. Notion helps me capture those ephemeral moments that would otherwise disappear.

![Monica Lim](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2FrYYRWHCyO9E98uHFLsoKE%2F85dba31096870315cd62fbe60c5fdacf%2FMonica_Lim.jpeg&w=2048&q=75)

Monica Lim  
Creator

Organize your professional and personal life
--------------------------------------------

# November 7, 2023 – Notion 2.34: new enterprise features and languages

### Hover preview of a Notion page

You ever want to 👀 peek at a Notion page? Now you can hover to preview page content.

### Notion is now available in 5 additional languages

You can use Notion in Danish, Dutch, Finnish, Norwegian, and Swedish. Set your preferred language in `Settings & members` → `Language & region`.

![nordics](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F5QF07nlEBdqz72MIeC5iZG%2Ff49a1e2adac290e8aa35fe46a793eb28%2Fnordics.png&w=3840&q=75)

### More visibility & controls to manage users

New dashboard and controls help you manage users with your domain.

# Freelance marketer Luri Lee manages personal and professional projects in Notion

Freelance marketer Luri Lee manages personal and professional projects in Notion
================================================================================

Luri Lee has a lot going on — she's a freelance marketer, content creator, and avid traveler. With Notion, she keeps all her initiatives on track, whether she's managing her personal life, like her budget, or building her business.
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Insights from

![](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F32P2uhFwODypYsB3kXe2AU%2Ff0bf395e2e04128eb1ecd73753569f6c%2Fluri-lee__1_.png&w=96&q=80)

Luri Lee

Freelance marketer

Use cases

Personal Organization

1

A portfolio that's easy to build, share, and update
---------------------------------------------------

After 8 years at an IT startup as a marketer and planner, Luri recently launched her own business. As a newly minted freelancer, she needed to spin up a portfolio to showcase her past work and attract new clients. Notion gives her a quick, no-fuss way to display all her work, like projects, her e-book, and YouTube episodes publicly on a website. She can even select only the parts of her portfolio that she wants to disclose — if a project is still under construction, she can make that specific page private until she's ready to share.

Hosting her portfolio in Notion also signals to her potential clients in the startup space that she's dialed into the latest technologies.

![Magazine](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F5Plb506MZyi9KqeghOMOmy%2F2029c894c60e07f5dd51b9c4dc97861b%2Fmagazine.png&w=1080&q=75)

Luri’s portfolio shares all her experience with potential clients.

![](https://www.notion.com/front-static/pages/case-studies/quote-left-rectangle.png)

Notion is so convenient for a freelancer. I can just write, share the page, and it looks like a proper website.

![Luri Lee](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F32P2uhFwODypYsB3kXe2AU%2Ff0bf395e2e04128eb1ecd73753569f6c%2Fluri-lee__1_.png&w=2048&q=75)

Luri Lee  
Freelance marketer

2

Spending and saving tracked for long-term financial success
-----------------------------------------------------------

Luri doesn't just use Notion for her professional projects — she also runs her entire personal life on the app, including all her finances. To keep track of how much money is in each bank account, she records her list of accounts in a table and connects it to a list of scheduled withdrawals. That way, she knows exactly how much is coming in, how much is going out, and where it's coming from.

Luri even set up a dashboard with summaries of her income, savings, and expenses. She can see the state of her finances in one place, stay within her budget, and hit her savings goals.

![Post-it Illustration](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2FaFzOEOD3xueO4CMbYNJVW%2F73e45a9211eed5391023caa61cc7b72f%2Fpost-its.png&w=1080&q=75)

Luri manages all her finances using Notion dashboards.

![](https://www.notion.com/front-static/pages/case-studies/quote-right-oval.png)

Before, it was hard to track my different bank accounts and make sure I was paying bills on time. In Notion, I can see it all at a glance.

![Luri Lee](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F32P2uhFwODypYsB3kXe2AU%2Ff0bf395e2e04128eb1ecd73753569f6c%2Fluri-lee__1_.png&w=2048&q=75)

Luri Lee  
Freelance marketer

3

A travel log to keep memories, plans, and recommendations in one place
----------------------------------------------------------------------

When she’s not creating content, Luri is traveling around the globe. She uses a Notion database to document all the places she’s been and to plan where she’s going next. For each trip, she tags the country and the people she traveled with. Clicking on each trip opens a page with notes and reflections that she can look fondly upon later.

At the heart of all her trips are the people who share these memories with her. With one click, Luri can see the groups of people she spent the most time with on each trip. And when it’s time to go somewhere new, she invites her family and friends to her Notion database so they can plan together.

![Globe Illustration](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F2H7lnx3NNrpdNlKBOU62ME%2F17ede796460c3ce663b687a928043e17%2Fglobe.png&w=1080&q=75)

Luri keeps track of all her trips (and who they were with) in one database.

![](https://www.notion.com/front-static/pages/case-studies/quote-left-blob.png)

I had so much information scattered across so many tools. Now everything's together in Notion.

![Luri Lee](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F32P2uhFwODypYsB3kXe2AU%2Ff0bf395e2e04128eb1ecd73753569f6c%2Fluri-lee__1_.png&w=2048&q=75)

Luri Lee  
Freelance marketer

# Three personal websites, all built with Notion

Three personal websites, all built with Notion
==============================================

Anabella, Corbin, and Temirlan all created Notion pages and made them accessible to anyone online — a resume, portfolio, and complete agency website. Other website makers were too cumbersome and hard to update. Now, they're using Notion pages that are easily customized and distributed with a single click.
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Insights from

![](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2FnG9nh9mTHhM43Rnnk50Yf%2F7d670660083f938b85134af278daa405%2Fanabella-spinelli.png&w=96&q=80)

Anabella Spinelli

Software engineer

![](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F4Niaf9n6tFJfPUEDiXUe4f%2F8ef9982e179dcc43a6e79a60eddd4107%2Ftemirlan-nugmanov.png&w=96&q=80)

Temirlan Nugmanov

Founder

![](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F5ZRElwiySICIogXCUeur5v%2F44fd2dc6aaacfdf0cea41982deb5cf2a%2Fcorbin-bell.png&w=96&q=80)

Corbin Bell

Graphic designer

Use cases

Personal Websites

1

A resume that's tailored to meet Anabella's needs
-------------------------------------------------

When Barcelona-based software engineer Anabella started searching for jobs, a static PDF didn't feel like it reflected her accurately. She wanted something that was intuitive to her tech background, met her design standards and would make it easy to add new projects or experience. So she used Notion to build her resume and simply shared the link in her applications.

![tools](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2FLcw9UWJuoU29oL3483rp7%2Fc3326fb2e77de42fef57a90c3d198060%2Ftools.png&w=1080&q=75)

Anabella can easily update her resume with new work or experience.

### The exact structure she wanted

Using headers, Anabella guides viewers through her background. Her experience, contact info and skills are neatly bundled — complete with splashes of personality authentic to her. She even has sections for languages she speaks, and links to blog posts (she's an avid writer). With a public Notion page as her resume, Anabella can make sure she's always putting her best foot forward.

![](https://www.notion.com/front-static/pages/case-studies/quote-left-rectangle.png)

You can effortlessly make pages look good in Notion. Its design system is a great framework, and is also flexible enough to be customized.

![Anabella Spinelli](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2FnG9nh9mTHhM43Rnnk50Yf%2F7d670660083f938b85134af278daa405%2Fanabella-spinelli.png&w=2048&q=75)

Anabella Spinelli  
Software engineer

2

Graphics, images and videos make Corbin's portfolio dynamic
-----------------------------------------------------------

A photographer, videographer, and graphic designer, Corbin is a multi-disciplinary artist who needed a portfolio for all the different types of work he does. Every website builder he tried required too much upfront knowledge and time, and made it difficult to add new pieces. Instead, he uses a published Notion page as a living, breathing showcase of his design work.

![Magazine](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F5Plb506MZyi9KqeghOMOmy%2F2029c894c60e07f5dd51b9c4dc97861b%2Fmagazine.png&w=1080&q=75)

![Corbin embeds many different kinds of files in his visual portfolio with one click.](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F5E53ir488WxzhxHZ6qHnaD%2Fffe490995918c1d20dacb0f0dbca2f1b%2Fcorbin-portfolio.jpg&w=3840&q=75)

Corbin embeds many different kinds of files in his visual portfolio with one click.

### Galleries with a bit more power

Corbin uses the gallery feature to put all his work on display. Each project is a page, and inside those pages are details about the project and the work behind it. His photography is also in a Notion gallery, where each image is tagged with the location, type of camera, and editing software for more context. As a visual person, it's important for Corbin to put design at the forefront of his portfolio, while also providing granular details for those who want to learn more about his work.

![](https://www.notion.com/front-static/pages/case-studies/quote-right-oval.png)

Notion is like a set of building blocks. I love being able to show what I want to show, and display it in any format I want.

![Corbin Bell](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F5ZRElwiySICIogXCUeur5v%2F44fd2dc6aaacfdf0cea41982deb5cf2a%2Fcorbin-bell.png&w=2048&q=75)

Corbin Bell  
Graphic designer

3

Temirlan runs his agency with a Notion page as its website
----------------------------------------------------------

Temirlan has tested more software than most people have even heard of while studying entrepreneurship at NYU, working at several accelerators, and starting his own company. This knowledge of tools and company infrastructure led him to start his own consultancy, helping companies find the right software for their workflows.

![Growing books](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F77gr0HREFhGUx1f6FONmFA%2Fe27d6a0329cccae35e6ed1819c1b688a%2Fgrowing-books.png&w=1080&q=75)

![Temirlan runs his agency from a website he built right in Notion.](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F766dq8AY8j6pEAWv7aJytk%2Fade21e9bdc3f371d5ef59c12869737ba%2Ftermirlan.jpg&w=3840&q=75)

Temirlan runs his agency from a website he built right in Notion.

### A simple, flexible storefront on the web

Called Optemization, the agency has a public-facing website run entirely on Notion. The table of contents in the header makes navigation seamless between sections. And he has a page for each client, where the scope of the project is detailed and categorized using a series of tags to denote cost, time and tools used. He even runs the company's blog here. Using Notion, Temirlan was able to set up a whole business without writing a single line of code.

![](https://www.notion.com/front-static/pages/case-studies/quote-left-blob.png)

I centralize projects in Notion. It’s so easy to add a new one — you get the benefits of a web editor without the hassle.

![Temirlan Nugmanov](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F4Niaf9n6tFJfPUEDiXUe4f%2F8ef9982e179dcc43a6e79a60eddd4107%2Ftemirlan-nugmanov.png&w=2048&q=75)

Temirlan Nugmanov  
Founder

# Activist Amira Yahyaoui's structure for supreme organization

Activist Amira Yahyaoui's structure for supreme organization
============================================================

Amira Yahyaoui values organization. She's a Tunisian refugee, exiled from her home at 17, who started a globally-renowned nonprofit to fight for government accountability, and now a startup helping students apply for financial aid. Amira needed one place to control her how-do-you-get-all-that-done-in-24-hours life. Notion is her organizational framework.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Insights from

![](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F7n6dxtK7TleumPgS0B4f7v%2F41b1b0bdcf051cf83bee7066b1f1ae16%2Famira-yahyaoui.png&w=96&q=80)

Amira Yahyaoui

Activist & entrepreneur

Use cases

Personal Organization

1

A calendar that's not just dates.  
It's a habit tracker.  

------------------------------------------------------------

Amira keeps a calendar in Notion that holds her accountable for all her habits. Before, she'd pingpong between multiple apps and calendars to see if she actually did the habit she hoped to accomplish. Ironically, the back-and-forth took up so much time that it was impossible to get to everything.

Now, she's using a Notion calendar as her personal home base. Each day, she's able to trigger and track all her habits, like workouts and journal entries. This makes it possible for Amira to see all the things that matter to her in one place, know immediately that they got done, and see when they happened.

![Calendar](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F3nTrtSnf69ZkXvxHRfNK7I%2Ff7840b398618510b6f5d566f5914a7bc%2Fcalendar.png&w=1080&q=75)

![Amira’s calendar is like a habit tracker for what she wants to accomplish every day.](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F1GpAJZH97sUgBMkk1f5bMz%2Ffe3f887d0feedfdfb443cbc2d1ac6c16%2Famira1.png&w=3840&q=75)

Amira’s calendar is like a habit tracker for what she wants to accomplish every day.

![](https://www.notion.com/front-static/pages/case-studies/quote-left-rectangle.png)

I love using Notion's calendar because you don't need to build a tracker on top of it. It’s a huge advantage because everything is in one place.

2

Imagine not being stressed when you pack for travel
---------------------------------------------------

Amira's activism takes her around the globe. Try packing for dozens of trips per year. Most people get stressed prepping for a weekend getaway.

Amira uses a template in Notion for all the different types of trips she takes. A week in cold weather? She's got a template for that packing list. A couple days on the beach? You bet there's a packing template for that. Even her skincare routine has a Notion template. As a frequent traveler, this practice frees her from the stress of knowing what to bring. She just copies the template, makes sure she has everything, then she's off.

![Suitcase ](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F1nDeAAoeepbyEZZMKugsVY%2F8faa3e996ee5fcc39f1969340968f1b0%2Fsuitcase.png&w=1080&q=75)

Amira uses templates to make packing a breeze.

![](https://www.notion.com/front-static/pages/case-studies/quote-right-oval.png)

Notion lowered a lot of the effort I need to put into organization. It saves the time that goes into the work of being organized.

3

Custom pages to share what matters most
---------------------------------------

Amira left her home at age 17. Ever since, she's been trying to get her mother to catalog the Tunisian recipes from her youth that she swears she can still taste. Together, they're finally doing it in Notion.

She has one Notion page that acts as her recipe book. Within that page are sub-pages customized for each recipe. They're organized just like a cookbook, with photos, ingredients and instructions for each. And now, Amira and her mother are even bringing more cooks into the kitchen. They created a shareable Notion link to the recipe book, so her aunt can contribute family recipes too.

![paper airplane](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F32o7O1At0eKjaMF56vqC41%2F88945dd9e87856455696d3913ebe1ed0%2Fpaper-airplane.png&w=1080&q=75)

Amira catalogs recipes from her childhood with her mother.

![](https://www.notion.com/front-static/pages/case-studies/quote-left-blob.png)

Notion creates a connection between someone like me, who is a tech nerd, and someone like my mother, who isn't.

# Keeping Meeting Minutes and Tasks in One Place | Notion

Meeting notes & action items, ready to go.


==============================================

Respect everyone’s time by maintaining meeting transcripts and relevant task lists in one place.

[Get Notion free](https://www.notion.com/signup)

![Centralize meeting notes and action items so everyone’s informed and prepared](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F3XNSJmSIU3nVTfK68itpXT%2F385162e0c9e9bb05fa53e014f50e8678%2Fimage.png&w=3840&q=75)

Capture every idea and action item in one place.


----------------------------------------------------

Meetings bring teams together to ask questions, generate solutions, build relationships, and adjust strategies. But these sessions are only effective if you’re tracking and sharing everyone’s ideas, comments, and action items. Create great meeting records, so the whole team feels informed and involved.

![A table that shows team meetings along with attendees and meeting types](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2FZncWSlUZePW5gZOAGToN8%2Ffd258c5ad65945337538581ac2775324%2Fimage.png&w=3840&q=75)

Collaborate in real time and let Notion handle the busywork.


----------------------------------------------------------------

Streamline your meeting process with Notion by creating and sharing notes with teammates in real time. You can unify meeting records alongside project management tools, while using AI to automatically extract action items and key information from meeting minutes.

![TBD](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F4sPu67UTSyzHIavRmIzyiS%2Fca8c4596d577dac36ec8fbc776a8da85%2Fimage.png&w=3840&q=75)

How to take meeting notes with Notion

To get started, create a Notion account. Then navigate to a new page and use a meeting notes template or type /meet. Once your meeting is recorded, you can use Notion AI to transcribe the content, generate a summary, or create a to-do list of action items.

==========================================================================

Transform simple meeting notes into actionable items, timelines, and visualizations in your Notion workspace.

[Get Notion free](https://www.notion.com/signup)

Questions & answers
-------------------

What are good check-out questions for meetings?

One tip for an effective meeting is to ensure you’re asking the right check-out questions. Some examples include:

*   How did you feel about the meeting?
    
*   Could you summarize the takeaways?
    
*   Do you have suggestions for future improvements?
    

What’s the value of team meetings?

Team meetings provide an opportunity for coworkers to collaborate, communicate, and align their goals. This leads to greater productivity, improved decision-making, and better team morale.

What are the different types of meetings?

Different types of corporate meetings include:

*   Team meetings
    
*   One-on-one meetings
    
*   All-hands meetings
    
*   Stand-up meetings
    
*   Brainstorming sessions
    
*   Training sessions
    
*   Virtual meetings
    

[Find a template](https://www.notion.com/templates/category/meetings)
 that works best for you and your organization.

How do I use Notion to take notes in meetings?

Taking notes in meetings in Notion is easy. All you need to do is:

1.  Create a Notion account.
    
2.  [On a new page](https://www.notion.com/help/create-your-first-page)
    , use a [meeting notes template](https://www.notion.com/templates)
     or type `/meet`. Create a new heading for each meeting agenda section and enter notes in bullet form.
    
3.  Invite teammates to edit in real time.
    
4.  Use Notion AI to transcribe, generate a summary or identify action items as a to-do list.
    

How can AI help me be more effective in meetings?

AI-powered meeting notes in Notion help you stay focused on your conversations by automatically transcribing, organizing, and summarizing your meetings—so you never miss important takeaways or action items. With features like real-time transcription, instant summaries, and searchable history, Notion AI ensures all your meeting notes are easy to find and ready to share with your team.

Learn more about using [AI meeting notes](https://www.notion.com/product/ai-meeting-notes)
 to boost your meeting productivity.

# How the world's fastest-growing startup stays fast with Notion

![Cursor](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F6SjSLOEn5vfK6rYcigGkY6%2F9e6c9d713a817654b43cd184ad1883cb%2FCursor_logo_small.png&w=640&q=75)

How the world's fastest-growing startup stays fast with Notion
==============================================================

Cursor became the fastest-growing company in history by giving developers AI superpowers. But scaling from a few founders to 100+ people threatened to slow them down with meetings, misfit tools, and lost knowledge. They turned Notion into their lean “OS”—where AI answers questions instantly, new hires onboard themselves, and every team builds exactly what they need. The result: 5x fewer tools, zero information silos, and builders who stay focused on building.
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Insights from

![](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F6UDph3FMKbYrnCe95kylQI%2Ffdd7e7a54e6a10c1b12eddade96e2f9f%2FMichael.png&w=96&q=80)

Michael Truell

Co-founder & CEO

![](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2FQuRlvc5RjleR2K6nRWI5Z%2F21ff39cb6ca1158ddd03d30eb638a1eb%2FRyo.png&w=96&q=80)

Ryo Lu

Head of Design

Use cases

Notion AI, Company-wide use

1

Simplicity solves for the complexities of scale
-----------------------------------------------

Most fast-growing companies slow down as they scale because they add the wrong things: specialized tools that create silos, meetings to coordinate between those silos, and more processes to manage the complexity they've created. Cursor did the opposite.

When your AI code editor is changing how millions of developers work, every internal meeting about "process" is time not spent shipping. Every tool that requires context-switching is cognitive overhead your builders can't afford. **Cursor's founders didn't want their builders to become managers of work.**

"It's really important for the people that are building to have a lot of time to build, and not be stuck in tons of meetings or just work about work," says Cursor CEO and co-founder Michael Truell.

They started with Notion because it could grow with them and give everyone AI tools to avoid busywork. Over time, Notion has evolved into a lean operating system—a single space where all knowledge lives and work is done.

![Brick wall cursor](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F5s1NBLrbltaCp9s8PJFAJP%2F4bda36aa5f0b92a34c9a402f52afdf3e%2FBrick_wall_cursor.png&w=1080&q=75)

![](https://www.notion.com/customers/cursor)

2

Why they build instead of buy
-----------------------------

While Cursor's rapid growth has captured headlines over the past few years, the majority of their first 100 employees are foundational hires made just in the last year. This created a pivotal moment in their growth. They needed to establish shared ways of working focused on actual workflows rather than purchasing point solutions and specialized tools for every function. "We were able to start with a default wiki," explains Michael, "and have each team build out their own business processes into things like databases."

This organic approach is core to Cursor's unique philosophy for scaling a company—instead of standardizing processes, they standardized the foundation and **let each team build and refine exactly what they need in their Notion workspace:**

*   **Engineering and research** maintain connected roadmaps and project trackers through databases that give leadership visibility while letting individual contributors focus on deep work—eliminating the need for standup meetings and the endless "when will this ship?" pings in Slack.
    
*   **Design and product** manage their entire development workflow from initial concepts to launch planning in one place, with the ability to directly embed anything from code to mockups to other apps like a Loom video to explain or a Miro board to ideate.
    
*   **Sales and customer success** reps like Jonathan Braude describe Notion as “the only tool that can keep up with the pace of change” at the company. It gives them a place to develop playbooks and lightweight systems they can iterate on as their teams grow, capturing insights from every customer interaction that the rest of the company can access—all without having to log into a separate CRM.
    

What emerged wasn't just efficiency but transparency by design. When teams build custom workflows on shared infrastructure, customization creates visibility rather than isolation. Each team's specialized systems become windows into their work rather than walls around it.

![Building paper cutout](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F1F1MYPTxpiR1ayTOsz9Ap8%2F9affcf8f65afc4724ebfb75b68e4b85c%2FBuilding_paper_cutout.png&w=1080&q=75)

![](https://www.notion.com/front-static/pages/case-studies/quote-left-rectangle.png)

I honestly can't imagine running a design team without Notion. It's our everything-but-code layer—we use it for project planning, design specs, research, to-dos. We have databases for just about everything.

![Ryo Lu](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2FQuRlvc5RjleR2K6nRWI5Z%2F21ff39cb6ca1158ddd03d30eb638a1eb%2FRyo.png&w=1080&q=75)

Ryo Lu  
Head of Design

3

AI is strongest where work flows and knowledge grows
----------------------------------------------------

Fast-moving teams don’t have time to dig for information, but most knowledge management tools weren’t built for the velocity or possibility of AI.

Cursor gets this. They know that to unlock AI’s potential, they needed a system that can capture everything happening at the company then make that knowledge easily accessible. Notion AI is that connective tissue—it brings together everything across their Notion workspace and all the tools like Slack that are connected to it to surface what their people need:

*   **Onboarding without friction**: Notion AI serves as an onboarding buddy for new hires, surfacing relevant documents and context they didn't even know existed. No need to overwhelm teammates with questions or schedule catch-up meetings.
    
*   **Search that saves hours**: Using Enterprise Search, teams get immediate answers from Notion AI instead of hunting through docs and messages. With all organizational knowledge centralized, hard to reach information no longer slows them down.
    
*   **Real-time updates, no standups required**: Anyone can stay current on project progress by asking Notion AI, which surfaces related work and dependencies across teams that individuals might never have discovered on their own.
    

![NosyGreeting](https://images.ctfassets.net/spoqsaf9291f/Tiw6yFj7yxptJGfDd0T7h/ba69d753422e8459ab109b3d1fea8be9/NosyGreeting.gif)

With Notion AI built into where Cursor teams work and collaborate, the friction between knowing and doing dissolve. It connects dots across their workspace + connected apps, delivering rich insights and context right where work flows and decisions happen.

![](https://www.notion.com/front-static/pages/case-studies/quote-right-oval.png)

Keeping people in the loop as we’ve grown so fast is really difficult, but Notion AI helps everyone get the latest updates and context of what’s going on across the company instantly.

![Michael Truell](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F6UDph3FMKbYrnCe95kylQI%2Ffdd7e7a54e6a10c1b12eddade96e2f9f%2FMichael.png&w=1080&q=75)

Michael Truell  
Co-founder & CEO

4

The modern stack: 5x fewer tools required
-----------------------------------------

Where most companies accumulate tools that slow them down, Cursor consolidated to stay fast. Instead of buying a docs editor, an AI note-taking app, specialized knowledge bases, project management software, and enterprise search tools to stitch them all together, they get it all in one connected AI workspace.

This consolidation delivers real savings: **Cursor estimates their current setup would have cost 5x more** if assembled from individual point solutions. But the real win isn't financial—it's that everyone can actually find what they need without jumping between five different apps. With a leaner approach to how their teams operate, everyone avoids the context-switching that kills momentum and gains clarity often elusive at high-growth companies.

For a company building the future of code, their tools had to match their philosophy: simple, accessible, and smart enough to handle the boring stuff. As they continue to scale, Cursor recently moved onto Notion's Enterprise plan for the advanced controls, security, and dedicated support they need. Cursor doesn't just move fast on Notion—they stay aligned, stay focused, and keep building what matters most.

![Pocket knife tools](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F01ax0ESlzwtYXzu1SqT4ky%2F2d9f2756ec798a98a9450c4ab09be7e5%2FPocket_knife_tools.png&w=1080&q=75)

![](https://www.notion.com/front-static/pages/case-studies/quote-left-blob.png)

We’re moving away from a world where communication is siloed towards one where everything’s accessible, and people up and down the org chart can contribute. Notion helps you do that in a low-overhead way.

![Michael Truell](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F6UDph3FMKbYrnCe95kylQI%2Ffdd7e7a54e6a10c1b12eddade96e2f9f%2FMichael.png&w=1080&q=75)

============================================================

At the scale of a $5B global wholesale platform, tools multiply fast and leading the industry means adopting the latest AI even faster. But Faire overcomes sprawl and silos with their Notion workspace, which keeps plans, work, and the decisions behind them connected. The result: workflows run over 30% faster, teams reclaim an average of 9 hours weekly, and AI agents inherit years of rich business context.

1

Making momentum legible
-----------------------

Faire connects independent brands and retailers worldwide. Inside the company, work needed to connect just as powerfully across people, workstreams, and tools—all moving at full speed. That pace demands rigor. Without it, teams hunt for context across Slack threads, Jira tickets, GitHub repos, hundreds of Notion pages, and more. Contributors need the latest work and the reasons behind it. Leaders need to see what matters without slowing anyone down. AI can help, but it’s even more powerful when it meets the work with real context.

The challenge: maintain momentum without losing the story behind each decision. Faire’s answer was to make that story visible from the moment work begins—and keep it legible as priorities shift, projects evolve, and teams scale.

Notion started as Faire’s living memory. As teams grew, it evolved into the nerve center organizing context, linking workflows, and shortening the path from “what’s going on?” to “what’s next?” That foundation meant AI didn’t start cold—it reasoned over real plans, rituals, and records.

![Writing tools gears](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F2lToTnIzFBJWX0so10KaPz%2F0e624a4b20491c343d71d381a8993565%2FWriting_tools_gears.png&w=1080&q=75)

![](https://www.notion.com/front-static/pages/case-studies/quote-left-rectangle.png)

A well-designed tool isn’t just for show—it’s leverage. Notion’s thoughtful design speeds up collaboration and decisions so we can deliver impact to our customers faster.

![Renee Solorzano](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F14joOtMuGtmqU2PZAcYajL%2F3d6b8bbae199fecbc2993088ebc9696a%2FRene_Solorzano.png&w=1920&q=75)

2

When tools think together, work flows differently
-------------------------------------------------

Specialized tools can create silos that make collaboration feel heavy. Faire solved this by making Notion the connective tissue across their tech stack. Notion AI made everything truly connected by including years of workspace knowledge plus context from 20+ integrated work apps.

**Engineering, Product, and Design** teams connect their entire product-development lifecycle in Notion—roadmaps, PRDs, and sprints in linked databases that sync with Jira, GitHub, and Figma. **GTM teams** tie initiatives and resources directly to product work, so updates cascade automatically.

When Notion AI generates updates or answers questions, it draws from all these connected sources plus years of conversations across Slack and email. Less context-switching. More shipping. And AI that knows the full story behind every decision.

![Toolbox](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F66TYnGEkuexYsQ2fbMcaSP%2F5b259e137df71b03a27074a26bb60f22%2FToolbox.png&w=1080&q=75)

By automating the routine work, we can create space for deep work and innovative bets. Leveraging AI isn't losing control—it's gaining time to make better decisions for customers.

AI is most powerful where the knowledge grows and the work flows

**Why 71% of employees surveyed say Notion AI is their most valuable AI tool:**

*   One place to ask, act, and align, instead of hopping between AI apps and systems of record
    
*   Trusted answers with citations pulled from connected knowledge and integrated tools
    
*   Immediate leverage from years of structured information other point tools couldn’t reach
    

In practice, this approach lets Faire reduce reliance on point solutions while reclaiming enough time to realize an estimated **8× ROI** on their Notion Enterprise plan.

Ashley Brasier

4

From AI experimentation to AI habits that stick
-----------------------------------------------

An initial AI hackathon across 30+ teams sparked possibilities. Then Faire built something more systematic: an AI-first enablement model with a dedicated AI Enablement leader and embedded “AI Captains” on every team. Captains don’t just train people—they coach in context, ship playbooks, and help teams redesign their actual workflows so AI removes friction instead of creating new steps.

_What other companies can learn from Faire’s approach:_

*   **Connect before you add**. Organize knowledge and workflows in a connected workspace first, integrating Slack, Google Drive, Jira, email, and more—AI that meets real context beats AI starting from scratch every time.
    
*   **Empower captains to lead change**. Name AI champions per function with a simple charter: pilot new ways of working and share the learnings. Meet on a regular cadence and keep shared templates and wins where teams can access them.
    
*   **Train on live workflows, not decks**. Sessions anchored in teams’ actual databases and templates beat generic AI training—people learn by improving work they already own.
    
*   **Build the loop, not the event**. Help channels, bi-weekly showcases, and live-workflow training create continuous cycles where breakthroughs spread and stick.
    
*   **Aim goals at customer impact, not work activity**. Track cycle time and decision clarity in your actual work, not training-completion rates. Each team owns their own AI-related OKRs targeted at their team’s greatest opportunity areas.
    

![Lightbulb air balloon](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F4oxlxRa63033PlJRKmRffQ%2F2d7a88e9c1a2afa3b53129cdf77bc792%2FLightbulb_air_balloon.png&w=1080&q=75)

5

30% faster workflows and hours back every week
----------------------------------------------

With years of connected knowledge and clean workflows in Notion, AI acts with full context rather than starting from scratch. Teams use Notion AI to generate meeting notes, synthesize customer feedback, draft documentation, and find answers across their workspace and all the tools they have connected to it.

_Here’s what changed for Faire:_

*   **81% say meetings run smoother** as Notion AI captures notes and action items, letting people focus on discussions and decisions.
    
*   **Workflows run over 30% faster** with everything centralized in one connected hub where AI applies full-context reasoning and automates next steps.
    
*   **Teams save an average of 9 hours per week** as AI not only finds answers but surfaces insights teams wouldn’t know to search for.
    

![Laptop line chart](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F2mXCaCnZ7oPxjWZBlBNgPF%2F9d4850b2f00d64fe5c3f36a3ce80304d%2FLaptop_line_chart.png&w=1080&q=75)

At Faire, AI is a key lever for working faster and smarter. We're weaving it into daily workflows, giving teams more time for high-impact work for our customers. What's especially exciting is discovering new ways to use it daily, and we're just getting started.

Thuan Pham  
CTO

6

When agents inherit 7+ years of context
---------------------------------------

Faire’s AI transformation shows that preparing for the next era of AI isn’t about waiting for perfect technology—it’s about organizing work so agents can be effective from day one. Years of organized knowledge mean AI agents won’t arrive as strangers trying to figure everything out.

“Our workspace is already organized. Agents fit right in by doing the assembly work so teams jump straight into decision making, instead of wasting time looking for context,” says Brasier.

By connecting the company’s planning, workflows, and knowledge in Notion, Faire has created a workspace where AI amplifies existing habits rather than disrupts them.

For Faire, the foundation is set: **94% of employees say Notion is “mission-critical”** to their work. Now, Notion AI feels like a set of teammates who’ve been there all along.

![Nosey Agent](https://images.ctfassets.net/spoqsaf9291f/5qcSkQZDgqxYgi5adurYM5/31f58f3884dbaecf4a73233546a5cc00/NoseyHeadset.gif)

![](https://www.notion.com/front-static/pages/case-studies/quote-left-oval.png)

We have seven years of work in Notion. It’s our workspace for essentially everything. AI with Agents builds on that, so we move from idea to action faster.

# Why we built Notion – About

About Notion
============

A story of tools and the future of work
---------------------------------------

![An illustration of several people working on computers at their desk](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=%2Ffront-static%2Fpages%2Fabout%2Fabout-01-v2.png&w=2048&q=75)

Hi there! If you’re reading this, you’re probably like me—spending most of your days in your office, in front of a computer.

![An illustration of a person leaving their chair and walking away](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=%2Ffront-static%2Fpages%2Fabout%2Fabout-02.png&w=2048&q=75)

You probably have fifteen tabs open: one for email, one for Slack, one for Google Docs, and on, and on…But have you ever thought about where these “work tools” came from? Or why there are so many of them?To answer these questions, and to explain why we created Notion, we have to travel back in time.

![A photo of people working in a factory during the industrial revolution](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=%2Ffront-static%2Fpages%2Fabout%2Fabout-03%2Fen-US.png&w=2048&q=75)

As people flooded to factories during the Industrial Revolution, many tools were invented to ease the management overload.Typewriters replaced illegible handwriting. File cabinets stored more information than any clerk could remember (and also won the gold medal at the 1893 Chicago World’s Fair!)Tools like these paved the way for offices as we know them today.

![A photo of people working at a large computer in the mid 20th century](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=%2Ffront-static%2Fpages%2Fabout%2Fabout-04.png&w=2048&q=75)

Fast forward to the 1950s, when computers came into the workplace.At first, they were lifeless, room-sized monsters that operated on paper and punch cards. They were used solely for number-crunching.

![An illustration of a person celebrating several documents](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=%2Ffront-static%2Fpages%2Fabout%2Fabout-05.png&w=2048&q=75)

Then something magical happened in the 1970s!A generation of pioneers saw the computer as something far beyond a number-crunching machine. They dreamt a future where computers could amplify imagination (Alan Kay), augment intellect (Doug Engelbart), and expand our thoughts far beyond text on paper (Ted Nelson).A tool unlike anything we have seen before.

![An illustration of a person sitting on top of a video player](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=%2Ffront-static%2Fpages%2Fabout%2Fabout-06.png&w=2048&q=75)

![](https://www.notion.com/about)

Play

Uh-oh! It looks like your ad blocker is preventing the video from playing.

Please watch it on [YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BFlop4sP8Os)

![](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=%2Ffront-static%2Fillustrated-icons%2FcatLookingUp.png&w=384&q=75)

But things didn’t turn out that way. As the legend goes, when Steve Jobs “borrowed” the ideas behind personal computing from Xerox PARC, many subtle, but crucial, differences were lost. Hit play and listen to the man himself.

![An illustration of type writers and filing cabinets with a person shrugging in confusion about these items](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=%2Ffront-static%2Fpages%2Fabout%2Fabout-07-v2%2Fen-US.png&w=2048&q=75)

And that’s where we are today.Google Docs made typewriters multiplayer. Dropbox brought file cabinets to the cloud. But conceptually, they evolved little beyond their Industrial Revolution ancestors.We duct-tape everything together with emails, copy/paste, and countless open tabs.

![An illustration of a person taming a floating mess of documents](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=%2Ffront-static%2Fpages%2Fabout%2Fabout-08.png&w=2048&q=75)

That’s where Notion comes in. We want to break away from today’s tools—and bring back some of the ideas of those early pioneers.As a first step, we are blending much of your workflow into an **all-in-one workspace**. Want a task list? A product roadmap? A design repository? They are now all in one place. You can even customize your own workspace from dozens of building blocks.Solve your problems your way, bounded only by your imagination.

![An illustration of several people working at their desks in excitement and waving](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=%2Ffront-static%2Fpages%2Fabout%2Fabout-09.png&w=2048&q=75)

Hopefully you’ve gotten a good sense of why we started Notion. Challenging the status quo is not an easy task. Check out the product! Write in! We need early adopters like you to start a movement. See you soon!

**“The best way to predict the future is to invent it.”**  
Alan Kay, computing pioneer

* * *

Join us
-------

Notion is based in beautiful downtown San Francisco, with offices around the world. We are a diverse group of people interested in computing, history, art, alternative programming languages, and skateboarding.

Interested in joining us? [Learn more here](https://www.notion.com/careers)
 👈

* * *

# How dbt Labs avoided years of SaaS costs and scaled organizational knowledge with Notion AI

![dbt Labs](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F16rqm6ftSxVgTuwmAOtY61%2Fec7b2521935a2d8c3cb89efafd6d3189%2Fdbt-labs-signature_tm.png&w=640&q=75)

How dbt Labs avoided years of SaaS costs and scaled organizational knowledge with Notion AI
===========================================================================================

Globally distributed teams at dbt Labs are getting their work done faster and more collaboratively than ever before using the next generation of Notion AI. Now, by more efficiently leveraging a vast array of knowledge chronicling every meeting, spec, decision, and plan from across their organization instantly, they’re able to focus on doing their best work, more together, at scale.
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Insights from

![](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F74cYtdFhCyTxD65qVhkq2b%2F95fabb1216bf18aeb0c8f3ba3c2f2232%2FFaith_Lierheimer_dbt_labs.png&w=96&q=80)

Faith Lierheimer

Senior Technical Instructor

![](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F50tZtITStSgeA5JHm2gUUG%2Fcfe3594806663597fada27c41a21ebc2%2FJerrie_Kumalah_dbt_labs_2.png&w=96&q=80)

Jerrie Kumalah

Resident Solutions Architect

![](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F2pUv0R6SwJQ5hkb5mE9uj6%2F88424c0f171498f347d30e3e57901247%2FLeigh_Staub_dbt_labs.png&w=96&q=80)

Leigh Staub

Director of Employee Experience

Use cases

Notion AI, Documentation, Knowledge Base, Project Management

![](https://www.notion.com/customers/dbt-labs)

Play

Uh-oh! It looks like your ad blocker is preventing the video from playing.

Please watch it on [YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3LXAfd8VBek)

![](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=%2Ffront-static%2Fillustrated-icons%2FcatLookingUp.png&w=384&q=75)

1

Everyone knows what anyone documents in Notion—but what happens when the team rapidly doubles in size?
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

In 2016, three former colleagues created a software platform called ‘dbt’ to help data engineers work like software developers. Documentation is a key component to how dbt supports organizations in delivering trusted data products with velocity. Built-in features like automatic documentation and visible lineage help data teams maintain data quality effortlessly, reuse existing code, and optimize data pipelines. As an open-source tool, dbt quickly became a key piece of data infrastructure for thousands of companies to create and disseminate their organizational knowledge. Today, dbt Labs (the creator of dbt) has catapulted to unicorn status with hundreds of employees in the last few years.

At the heart of dbt Labs' mission is the emphasis they put on their own employees contributing to the knowledge loop by “_thinking in public and defaulting to open source_.” This ethos finds a powerful echo in the strong culture of documentation they’ve worked hard to foster with their teams over the years. As a remote-first company with team members in multiple time zones across the world, Notion became their centralized, single source-of-truth for everyone to contribute to and iterate on shared organizational knowledge. This robust documentation is not just a byproduct of their teams’ work; it’s a key enabler.

But as their teams nearly doubled in size over the past year, the challenge became scaling a swell of documentation in parallel.

![Fishing For Docs](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F2nb1MtDB8sKFK77XfcLDDk%2F1ff6c21713e665aabfc59549f5b8529d%2FdbtLabsdocs.png&w=1080&q=75)

![](https://www.notion.com/front-static/pages/case-studies/quote-left-rectangle.png)

Documentation is the critical thread in the fabric of your organization. Without strong documentation, knowledge transfer and collaboration breaks down— and the fabric of the organization starts to untether.

![Leigh Staub](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F2pUv0R6SwJQ5hkb5mE9uj6%2F88424c0f171498f347d30e3e57901247%2FLeigh_Staub_dbt_labs.png&w=2048&q=75)

Leigh Staub  
Director of Employee Experience

2

Scaling a culture of documentation requires a personalized AI assistant
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

At the helm of the company’s globally distributed employee experience, Leigh Staub needed to rethink how documentation culture at dbt Labs would evolve with their rapidly growing teams.

Many employees didn’t know where to create or how to best maintain their documentation. That quickly led to information sprawl, where personal and company knowledge was spread far and wide across the dbt Labs workspace. Notion is an integral part of all of their teams’ workflows, but they needed to find a way to reliably reel in information from the far reaches of their workspace.

That’s where Notion AI came in. At the suggestion of their Customer Success Manager at Notion, Leigh enrolled dbt Labs in an alpha testing group to be among the first Notion customers to trial the newest Notion AI assistant features. “Almost overnight, when Notion AI was enabled, the power of our documentation was illuminated, like a lighthouse being turned on, solving for information sprawl in what was coming to be known as our Notion ocean” says Leigh.

![lighthouse](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2FzmMlaSrNrEAn4SV57uIuK%2F3079d4e0fe3a90b0999cb1c8265bdb87%2Flighthouse.png&w=1080&q=75)

Organizing company docs is hard. Notion AI goes beyond search—synthesizing everything across your workspace to give the best, fully contextual answer—so teams don’t need perfect information architecture, just capture work in Notion and ask.

![](https://www.notion.com/front-static/pages/case-studies/quote-right-oval.png)

As a new employee, I don’t know what I don’t know, so it’s really powerful when Notion AI surfaces organizational knowledge that I didn’t know to look for because I didn’t even know it existed.

![Faith Lierheimer](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F74cYtdFhCyTxD65qVhkq2b%2F95fabb1216bf18aeb0c8f3ba3c2f2232%2FFaith_Lierheimer_dbt_labs.png&w=2048&q=75)

Faith Lierheimer  
Senior Technical Instructor

3

Notion AI helps each team tap into company-wide knowledge to find answers and speed up their daily work
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Doubling down on their documentation culture with the power of Notion AI has had compounding effects for the daily workflows and impact of teams at dbt Labs, particular those that are customer-facing.

For example, Solutions Architects take notes on every customer engagement, including what’s working well for customers or where they need support. Instead of needing to hunt through pages and pages of notes, **Notion AI cleans up those notes and gives them perfect memory** going into their next engagement using all of those previous notes. Not only do those summaries help them better prepare for upcoming customer calls, they help inform other people and teams about what’s happening with their customer.

The Community team uses Notion to collect and organize product feedback that they hear from across the growing dbt community. But what happens to that feedback once it’s written down? With Notion AI, other teams throughout the org — like Product and Engineering — can now **leverage synthesized insights from the wealth of community feedback** that has been documented to make more informed decisions about what they should be prioritizing.

![dbt Labs PRD with Notion AI](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2FqQBdqaZEgvYTNnyeGZS3H%2F4001cac7574c76619aa71dfd52b3a1fa%2FScreenshot_2024-09-24_at_3.00.26%C3%A2__PM.png&w=3840&q=75)

Technical Instructors teaching their customers how to use the newest features of dbt Labs’ growing product portfolio have to stay up to date with a lot of information that’s sometimes specific to a very particular product area. Using Notion AI, they’re able to pull information from a particular doc or teamspace to **instantly incorporate the latest product information** into their lesson plans.

“Notion AI has transformed this aspect of my work by automating the information discovery process,” says Senior Technical Instructor Faith Lierheimer. “I can use Notion AI as my assistant to eliminate spending hours of searching for the right information, which not only makes me more efficient but improves the quality and relevance of my work with our community.”

![trees 1](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F1UOcF1JgO1Iv4XAg79nVJT%2F42983811cf058d96cf7bc0355413cd46%2Ftrees_1.png&w=1080&q=75)

Notion AI is everyone’s always-on teammate for instant insights. Customer-facing teams can seamlessly incorporate the latest details of new products into their work and generate ideas based on customer feedback documented throughout the dbt Labs workspace.

4

Less tech stack fragmentation lowers costs and empowers everyone with knowledge
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Companies can only be so productive when they’re spending too much time searching for information. The team at dbt Labs understood that challenge all too well, and wanted to proactively find a better way to work.

They also saved a ton of money on their tool stack in the process. By centralizing their organizational knowledge with strong documentation in Notion, dbt Labs has also been able to reduce or limit additional licenses for other team-specific tools, while also avoiding the need to purchase additional tools for enterprise AI or search altogether—**saving them over $35k on their tool stack.**

However, most people at dbt Labs say the real value of Notion AI ultimately goes beyond time saved searching or costs. It gives them the space to think bigger and more creatively, and spend time on more strategic work.

“More than anything, Notion AI just gives me a bit of breathing room. Since it can take care of the administrative grunt work, I’m able to focus on doing the work that I do best,” says Jerrie Kumalah, Resident Solutions Architect at dbt Labs.

Now, the knowledge that’s collected is no longer just Leigh’s or Faith’s or Jerrie’s. It’s dbt Labs’ shared knowledge, and it propels them forward faster than ever. Together.

![Boat](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F17Q0ANv6KIvNjiC9NNSIiS%2Fdcf9b7ab18de9f283766efc911aef409%2Fnew-landing-pages-mgmt.png&w=1080&q=75)

Scale your org’s knowledge with personalized AI
-----------------------------------------------

How other teams use Notion
--------------------------

[![Toyota](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F6F7pLYnvpEmjRsLmJiJ1rr%2Fa05c47ebc68ead5c932697aa127c2632%2FToyota-s_Notion.png&w=1920&q=75)\
\
> ![Kumiko Buma](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F6Pg8OxJbKF3LnwhZFOk0Yy%2F2f07d9ccca5a49b7408d34a4e3aaa054%2FToyota_Buma.png&w=256&q=75)\
> \

# Parloa scaled 5× while cutting software costs in half with Notion

![Parloa ](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F2fXL0kAT9pclEVLp3AA1F%2F9e363792f05d20ec24c2981f8d9a10b9%2FParloa_logo.png&w=640&q=75)

Parloa scaled 5× while cutting software costs in half with Notion
=================================================================

Agentic AI pioneer Parloa moved from a patchwork tools like Coda, Confluence, and others to one AI‑powered workspace. Notion gave every team the speed, context, and security they needed to grow from seed stage to global innovator with at least 50% of the previous software costs.
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Insights from

![](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F1GSrgmVDlmVZoybgaTxv6Q%2F45ab49df139fcefe7c77dccc69be2c74%2FKarimParloa.png&w=96&q=80)

Karim Dasouki

Head of IS&T Governance

![](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F6njdJE5RkEAtVmRApeDh1J%2Fd9679ee332a48ee899ab015945e9fe75%2FKevin_Chang_headshot.jpeg&w=96&q=80)

Kevin Chang

Sales Engineering Lead

![](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F1scwvGy9xzAjnvPhjyVLvW%2F8703576922306769492cd97335700156%2FInga_Nielsen_Parloa_headshot.jpeg&w=96&q=80)

Inga Nielsen

Talent Acquisition Lead

Use cases

Company-wide use, Knowledge Base, Employee Onboarding, Notion AI

![](https://www.notion.com/customers/parloa)

Play

Uh-oh! It looks like your ad blocker is preventing the video from playing.

Please watch it on [YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=lK1PhKxHV6g)

![](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=%2Ffront-static%2Fillustrated-icons%2FcatLookingUp.png&w=384&q=75)

1

Unified teams need unified tools
--------------------------------

Parloa’s agentic AI platform for customer experience helps businesses streamline customer service interactions in seconds. But for Parloa’s teams, finding answers to their own work questions used to be anything but instant. Project management lived in Asana, technical documentation in Confluence, and internal processes in Coda. Each application had its own structure and limited AI capabilities, making it difficult for teams to collaborate effectively. Finding context required tab‑hopping and Slack pings—time Parloa couldn’t spare as headcount and software costs multiplied.

Parloa’s leadership recognized that sustaining Parloa’s rapid growth required a more unified system that could bring work and teams together—enhanced by powerful AI. After thorough evaluation, Notion proved it would help them not only consolidate tools but fundamentally transform how they operate.

![tools](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F7ib3dWcEY1n9AEiQZoIfTS%2F282343ab2ff33358b1dc955d575a2718%2Ftools.png&w=1080&q=75)

![](https://www.notion.com/front-static/pages/case-studies/quote-left-rectangle.png)

Notion is the workspace we trust to deliver on the promise of AI. It’s how we give all our employees the superpower of AI search and automation.

![Karim Dasouki](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F1GSrgmVDlmVZoybgaTxv6Q%2F45ab49df139fcefe7c77dccc69be2c74%2FKarimParloa.png&w=1080&q=75)

Karim Dasouki  
Head of IS&T Governance

2

A workspace with a perfect memory
---------------------------------

Switching to Notion turned Parloa’s scattered data into living, searchable internal knowledge. Notion became the source of truth for every project, process, and document, and **Notion AI became the company’s collective memory**—not just for finding updates and content, but synthesizing answers with context pulled from Slack, Google Drive, and every page in between.

As Parloa’s teams experienced instant, context-aware answers in Notion, the ripple effects of their new system spread naturally across the company with teams using Notion AI in various ways. Now:

*   **Sales engineers move in lock‑step.** Product demos and discovery templates live in a centralized database. Reps can ask Notion AI questions like “What part of a demo matter most for healthcare clients?” and get instant, contextual answers. This has slashed prep work and communication overhead while ensuring consistent customer interactions, enabling new hires to join client meetings within their first month.
    
*   **An “Interview Academy” accelerates time-to-hire.** The talent acquisition team built an interactive training program that combines videos, quizzes, and AI-guided answers to any questions along the way. As a result, hiring cycles shortened and quality improved across departments, while employees discovered valuable organizational knowledge they wouldn't have found otherwise.
    
*   **Day‑to‑day work feels lighter.** Teams spend less time hunting for information and more time collaborating, as a single prompt to Notion AI surfaces perfectly synthesized insights from across their workspace and connected apps.
    

![](https://images.ctfassets.net/spoqsaf9291f/3IB6qonp3FP3irEFHI7qil/230b03a9272a34baa0a9214ec0c5bbe6/Sales_Enablement_Hub_v2_Notion_noborder.gif)

Parloa’s sales engineering team manages all product demos and discovery templates in a central Notion database. With Notion AI, they get instant, contextual answers to help prepare for customer meetings.

![](https://www.notion.com/front-static/pages/case-studies/quote-right-oval.png)

Notion AI streamlines our work by quickly surfacing the right documentation, which has been essential for maintaining our rapid growth.

![Kevin Chang](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F6njdJE5RkEAtVmRApeDh1J%2Fd9679ee332a48ee899ab015945e9fe75%2FKevin_Chang_headshot.jpeg&w=1920&q=75)

Kevin Chang  
Sales Engineering Lead

3

Cutting complexity and costs in half
------------------------------------

For Karim and Parloa’s IT team, switching to Notion wasn't just about finding a new tool—it was a strategic realignment of their digital infrastructure. By replacing a fragmented system of Coda, Confluence, and other tools with Notion and Notion AI, Parloa cut costs by 50% and reduced tool complexity. Notion’s [marketplace of templates](https://www.notion.com/templates)
 helped Parloa get up and running quickly, and its [native integrations](https://www.notion.com/integrations)
 with Parloa's existing tools prevent information from becoming stuck in specialized tools, inaccessible to the rest of the company. This foundation of organized, accessible information has been crucial as Parloa has expanded internationally, enabling them to maintain their ambitious pace of innovation.

![downgraph](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2FA6iPjrnAorPHQcigG7AfJ%2Fc7d3da2dd6454aa5b807cfcff7342ada%2Fdowngraph.png&w=1080&q=75)

![](https://www.notion.com/front-static/pages/case-studies/quote-left-blob.png)

Any company that wants to scale must consider how they'll transmit knowledge. With Notion, we've built an AI-powered workspace that’s designed for the future, not just for today.

![Inga Nielsen](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F1scwvGy9xzAjnvPhjyVLvW%2F8703576922306769492cd97335700156%2FInga_Nielsen_Parloa_headshot.jpeg&w=1920&q=75)

Inga Nielsen  
Talent Acquisition Lead

4

Security and flexibility built to scale
---------------------------------------

Parloa needed a workspace that could evolve alongside their business with enterprise-grade security requirements like SAML, granular permissions, and SCIM provisioning. [Notion delivered all these and more](https://www.notion.com/enterprise)
, plus a zero data retention AI policy—all in one single, flexible solution. As Parloa expanded from dozens to hundreds of employees, teams discovered new ways to connect workflows, share knowledge, and collaborate that previously would have required additional specialized tools.

**Bottom line:** With Notion, Parloa cut the costs of software sprawl in half and transformed scattered knowledge into their greatest asset. Teams spread across the globe now make better decisions, faster with AI-powered access to organizational knowledge, laying the foundation for Parloa's 5x employee growth—and beyond.

![](https://images.ctfassets.net/spoqsaf9291f/FNM8cG3jfRQQ4QPyzesGY/36e683c9a818bb9e74982a59ac2f3683/Parloa_OKRs20250514_noborder.gif)

Parloa’s Information Security & Technology team uses Notion to track OKRs with real-time dashboards, giving stakeholders instant visibility into progress.

![](https://www.notion.com/front-static/pages/case-studies/quote-right-rectangle.png)

Notion not only met all our security requirements, but also provided the flexibility and ease of use we needed to scale efficiently.

![Karim Dasouki](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F1GSrgmVDlmVZoybgaTxv6Q%2F45ab49df139fcefe7c77dccc69be2c74%2FKarimParloa.png&w=1080&q=75)

Karim Dasouki  
Head of IS&T Governance

# September 29, 2020 – September 29, 2020

### Write your notes by hand on iPad ✍️

The new "Scribble" feature for Apple Pencil works perfectly in Notion! Handwriting your notes instead of typing them can be a helpful boost for memorization — all you need to do is update to iPadOS 14 and you're good to go.

P.S. Sorry Android tablet users, this is more of an Apple feature than a Notion feature 😓 We'll keep making our mobile apps better across all platforms!

### Log out of Notion from all your devices, remotely 📡

This will come in handy if you forget to log out of your Notion account on a public computer, or your personal device is stolen or misplaced. Just go to `Settings & members` in your left-hand sidebar, go to the **My account** section, and click the button under `Log out of all devices`.

### Bug Fixes & improvements

*   Fixed a bug that caused the `Continue with Google` button to be unresponsive on mobile
    
*   Fixed a bug that caused inline equations to render as `Invalid equation` in PDF exports
    
*   Fixed a bug that prevented re-ordering of private pages in Enterprise workspaces where the `Prevent members from changing the Workspace section` setting was enabled
    
*   The four-randomly-generated-words in emails with a temporary passcode are now derived from High Valyrian instead of English to prevent awkward word combinations 😬
    

### Introducing: [Notion Guides](https://www.notion.so/guides)

Not sure what to build next? We just launched Notion Guides to serve up how-to posts and videos tailored to your needs! Find step-by-step tutorials to help you get the most from Notion. Plus, you can filter with tags like "Wiki," "Design," and "Engineering," to find exactly what you want.
