# Notion saves hundreds of hours and thousands of dollars for Viva

![Viva customer story](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F2a1i8ciBTR7KfXoh2VrceK%2Fd9610e8ffed7677fcbf7b4a5b48b3625%2FLOGO_BLACK_TRANSP_BACKGROUND.png&w=3840&q=75)

Notion saves hundreds of hours and thousands of dollars for Viva
================================================================

Viva hires, trains, and matches skilled remote executive assistants with fast-growing companies to help them get things done faster. Switching from other tools like Asana and Monday to run on Notion as their productivity hub not only saved them time and money, but also helps them save their customers an average of 8 hours per week.
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Insights from

![](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2FleWPnGzGkNkiRzsHfMIZp%2F9612cf5964ea54b8ff6eacaaa4287571%2FViva_Fineas_Tatar.png&w=96&q=80)

Fineas Tatar

Co-Founder & Co-CEO

![](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2Fz0zUgUcPBTsuxEb9GUEgy%2F85b9158bcb74fe3c094b468274168c23%2FViva_Sonia_Guzma_n_Franco.png&w=96&q=80)

Sonia Guzmán Franco

Customer Success Manager

Use cases

Knowledge Base, Employee Onboarding

1

The foundations of productivity gains: meeting people where they’re at
----------------------------------------------------------------------

Leaders at fast-growing companies juggle a ton of responsibilities that only continue to increase as the company grows, which often leads to them becoming less productive and more burnt out. Viva was founded to help these startup executives get a better handle on all that gets piled on their plate by matching them with executive assistants (EAs) they hire and continuously develop.

Running most of their business on Notion was deeply intertwined with the founders' vision to redefine workplace productivity. By combining top talent from Latin America with productivity strategies tailored to startup leaders, Viva aims to transform the role of executive assistants from mere administrative support to strategic partners. With so many startup executives running their businesses and personal life on Notion, Viva's approach begins by meeting executives in a familiar tool to better align their work and needs.

![be742d45f6ad870a8775dccf680437dc](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F581MvpKcwaatHhu8yVXLM4%2F4d627d016342924bc690867b137ed425%2Fbe742d45f6ad870a8775dccf680437dc.png&w=1080&q=75)

2

A connected workspace is a more collaborative one
-------------------------------------------------

Before adopting Notion, the Viva team was forced to use a mix of all the different tools their customers used, like the Google Suite, Asana, Monday, and Jira. The challenge, though, was that the tools were limited in function, and there weren't integrations to bring the tools together. This siloed approach meant work done in one tool often didn't connect with tasks in another. When everyone is using separate or multiple tools for work, collaborating and sharing of information is difficult. But once Viva consolidated everything into Notion, they unified all of their internal documentation and tasks previously scattered across multiple tools into one shared space.

With everything in Notion, knowledge became easier to access. This made onboarding new EAs into their workforce quicker, and helped make existing talent more effective. It also fostered greater collaboration and knowledge sharing among their EAs. For instance, they created a hub of different ways their EAs have used Notion with their customers, organized by problems solved, with key learnings, templates, and more. Tribal knowledge that is often locked way in one person’s mind is now accessible to everyone.

![Viva1](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F3v4L982AaveKsNwfwQZF8R%2F46bece2efd8e244f92e65203c33578f1%2FViva1.png&w=3840&q=75)

Viva's Resource Library in Notion serves as a comprehensive knowledge base designed to streamline the onboarding process for new team members and facilitate collaboration on best practices among existing EAs.

![](https://www.notion.com/front-static/pages/case-studies/quote-left-rectangle.png)

I was amazed by how much of our day-to-day work had shifted from several tools into one centralized hub in Notion. We went from a few folks using Notion for note-taking to the entire team using Notion to document, plan, and share information with each other.

![Sonia Guzmán Franco](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2Fz0zUgUcPBTsuxEb9GUEgy%2F85b9158bcb74fe3c094b468274168c23%2FViva_Sonia_Guzma_n_Franco.png&w=2048&q=75)

Sonia Guzmán Franco  
Customer Success Manager

3

A better way of working with customers too
------------------------------------------

Notion also offers an excellent platform for external collaboration with customers, which is why it’s particular popular among agencies and service providers like Viva.

For instance, Viva’s EAs manage all the notes, follow-ups, and tasks with each executive they support. Since so many of their customers already use Notion, Viva EAs become members in their shared workspace. If a customer works in other tool suites like Atlassian or Google, Viva creates a new Notion workspace to bring all this work together in a centralized hub using Notion’s integrations with many of these tools.

Companies like Viva even position their expertise in Notion and the customer hubs they create as part of their differentiated offering. "Our EAs use Notion as their main tool, which helps customer see its value firsthand," Viva’s co-founder Fineas Tatar explains. "Our ability to build and operate in Notion is now an extension of the service we provide."

![productivity plan](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2FmGCeyR4lg8DmRa0c3Yx0b%2Fd71c2da1d452e7c1d70fd061acea7b77%2FVivaProductivityPlan.png&w=3840&q=75)

Viva's creates productivity plans for all of their customers in Notion that serve as a centralized hub for all of the work their EAs support.

![](https://www.notion.com/front-static/pages/case-studies/quote-right-oval.png)

Beyond the time and money we’ve saved, the real value of Notion is how it’s transformed the way we work. Everyone at Viva leverages Notion in their own unique way, which empowers our teams and keeps our customers engaged.

![Fineas Tatar](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2FleWPnGzGkNkiRzsHfMIZp%2F9612cf5964ea54b8ff6eacaaa4287571%2FViva_Fineas_Tatar.png&w=2048&q=75)

Fineas Tatar  
Co-Founder & Co-CEO

4

Notion now saves Viva hundreds of hours of work and thousands of dollars on software
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

“Across the team, we’ve saved hundreds of hours of manual, repetitive work. And we keep on finding new creative ways to use the tool and save even more time," says Fineas. These productivity gains also get passed on to their customers. Viva’s team is able to increase the productivity of the executives they partner with by an average of 20%.

In addition to the time savings, Viva has also saved thousands of dollars on their tool stack by consolidating into Notion. This leaner tool stack ultimately lets Viva focus less on software and more on delivering the most effective productivity service to their customers.

![gift](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F1niCnKBCl4uVky3rOCIRJM%2Fe27c2edef77118580c47ec3ff29ded9d%2Fgift.png&w=1080&q=75)

Ready to power productivity like Viva?
--------------------------------------

How other teams use Notion
--------------------------

# Teams at Remote operate more efficiently by using Q&A to get instant answers to their questions 

![Remote](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F5D2MbIIXeHVKELRKMhmcV9%2F3b16a66282b99cf2322fdb606fdc61c6%2FRemote-logo.png&w=1200&q=75)

Teams at Remote operate more efficiently by using Q&A to get instant answers to their questions
===============================================================================================

Remote maintains a robust knowledge base for its 1,000+ global employees. Teams can now pull information from that knowledge base immediately with Q&A, promoting speed and focus.
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Insights from

![](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2FSPxAFg0CUZFYOTUGxkCp0%2Fdf3bb245834b87358fa9396413b0b440%2FSean_Stanley_Remote.png&w=96&q=80)

Sean Stanley

Internal Documentation

![](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F6V32C21r5V2UuZ6kFeJhyh%2Fa85b39385542e47bf0ad862223f041cb%2FTom_Bosse_Remote.png&w=96&q=80)

Tom Bosse

Senior Account Executive

![](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F4yNQNSqo4bW1nEQp580ybe%2Fe26f0245c2f2c24e4079c9232813fc2c%2FScott_Entwistle_Remote.png&w=96&q=80)

Scott Entwistle

Senior Recruiter

Use cases

Notion AI, Knowledge Base, Documentation

![](https://www.notion.com/customers/remote)

Play

Uh-oh! It looks like your ad blocker is preventing the video from playing.

Please watch it on [YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=uNqyVGjZFZc)

![](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=%2Ffront-static%2Fillustrated-icons%2FcatLookingUp.png&w=384&q=75)

1

Finding information quickly in their knowledge base
---------------------------------------------------

Remote is a global HR platform that helps companies hire and manage teams all over the world. The company has over 1,400 distributed employees itself, and maintains a robust knowledge base in Notion. It’s not just policies or processes. It’s also a huge amount of hyper-localized, country-specific information.

This information is central to every team, project, and workflow at Remote — whether it’s sales teams knowing benefits policies in different countries, or engineering teams pulling launch details from the product roadmap. But getting the information was so time consuming that Remote started building its own chatbot to make knowledge retrieval easier. They stopped when Notion introduced Q&A.

This new AI-powered feature helps Remote’s global employees get answers to their questions instantly — they’re saving time and operating more efficiently, instead of searching for the info they need to do their work.

2

One place where all teams go to answer their questions
------------------------------------------------------

When someone needs an answer to a question, where do they go? Often, there isn’t one person or one place people can go to unblock themselves. As a result, progress — no matter what you’re working on — stalls.

Remote previously used Google Docs and Asana. When they switched from Asana to Notion, Remote saved $485,000 per year. Notion is their single source of truth for everyone and everything. Static docs like company holidays. Dynamic, always-changing project updates and meeting notes. Other tools connect to Notion so it always holds the most up-to-date information. “We have a couple other software programs all feeding into Notion, so Notion is our one-stop shop,” says Sean Stanley, who leads Internal Documentation.

That means the answers to many questions can only be found in Notion. And if it lives in Notion, Q&A finds it. Instead of searching through different software, there’s one place that everyone at Remote turns to access critical knowledge. “Q&A is saving new employees days, if not weeks, of their onboarding to be able to find information quickly and learn from it,” says Scott Entwistle, Senior Recruiter.

Regardless of team or tenure, Notion holds answers to their questions. Q&A brings it to their fingertips.

Q&A gives instant answers to questions from your entire Notion workspace.

![](https://www.notion.com/front-static/pages/case-studies/quote-left-rectangle.png)

Q&A is the gateway to finding correct information. That’s something every organization needs.

![Tom Bosse](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F6V32C21r5V2UuZ6kFeJhyh%2Fa85b39385542e47bf0ad862223f041cb%2FTom_Bosse_Remote.png&w=2048&q=75)

Tom Bosse  
Senior Account Executive

3

Immediate access to the right knowledge — easier, faster, and more natural with Q&A
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Employees at Remote were seeking information multiple times a day, and getting answers to each of those questions took up to 10 minutes — whether it was searching their wiki or asking another employee and waiting for their response. With Q&A, they get answers in seconds.

“Q&A can change the landscape completely for any size company,” says Scott. “It’s been central to actually making sense of all the data we already have.” And Remote has a lot of data. Locating the correct Notion page was just the first step to answering a question — people still needed to read the page and sift out the relevant information.

Q&A can search across thousands of pages in seconds. It has the latest information. It knows the context. You can ask it a question in natural human language and get a response in human language, too. All of this makes getting knowledge easier, like you’re asking a coworker who has all the answers.

“The benefits of using Q&A for me are speed and efficiency,” says Andrew Pett, Staff Backend Engineer. “If I come across a keyword or some context I’m not familiar with, Q&A is my first port of call. It’ll search the relevant documents and present information back to me in a nice little summary.”

Q&A can provide clarity instantly, enabling teams at Remote to operate quicker because the vital knowledge that impacts their work is only a question (and a few seconds) away.

Q&A searches thousands of pages in seconds to provide answers to questions.

![](https://www.notion.com/front-static/pages/case-studies/quote-right-oval.png)

With Q&A, all you have to do is ask and it will bring the information to you. It’s saving everyone time and hassle.

![Sean Stanley](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2FSPxAFg0CUZFYOTUGxkCp0%2Fdf3bb245834b87358fa9396413b0b440%2FSean_Stanley_Remote.png&w=2048&q=75)

Sean Stanley  
Internal Documentation

4

Getting answers to questions in the flow of work, instead of wasting time context-switching
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Q&A is embedded right in Notion, so teams at Remote can ask questions and get answers as part of their existing workflows. Instead of searching for docs, pinging teammates, or scheduling meetings, they ask Q&A and get a response in seconds, enhancing focus and staying in the flow.

“I don’t have to hold as much information in my head. I don’t have to context switch. It reduces multitasking,” says Andrew. “And that’s a more productive world for me to live in as an engineer.”

Remote’s culture is built on documentation and async communication. It fosters more autonomous teams who know information exists, but searching through their knowledge base to find it is what’s disruptive. Q&A is changing that.

“Q&A makes documentation more usable,” says Scott. “We can just build up our knowledge, and Q&A can find the information we need.”

Information inside Remote’s documentation more easily accessible and usable with Q&A, creating a streamlined workflow for teams who previously wasted time searching for that info.

Employees use Q&A to get quick answers and stay in their workflow.

![](https://www.notion.com/front-static/pages/case-studies/quote-left-blob.png)

Q&A hugely improves my day-to-day work. I’m doing several searches per day, and it probably saves between 10 - 20 minutes.

![Scott Entwistle](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F4yNQNSqo4bW1nEQp580ybe%2Fe26f0245c2f2c24e4079c9232813fc2c%2FScott_Entwistle_Remote.png&w=2048&q=75)

Scott Entwistle  
Senior Recruiter

# PartnerStack consolidated project management tools into Notion, delivering value to customers faster

![PartnerStack](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F5VbXxSr7e1jwE0GHuZWIKP%2Fbe5f47c56ee440377f863c7153d16f50%2Fpartnerstack_logo.png&w=640&q=75)

PartnerStack consolidated project management tools into Notion, delivering value to customers faster
====================================================================================================

PartnerStack uses Notion to manage projects across the entire company. They consolidated project management tools into Notion — saving time and money, increasing shipping cadence, and fostering operational clarity.
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Insights from

![](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F2VX8DaTXP2MaFPKz6Geplv%2F9c4d2eea8839fe213884d4d55523fa4b%2FProject_Management_Tyler_Calder.png&w=96&q=80)

Tyler Calder

CMO

Use cases

Project management, Company-wide use

![](https://www.notion.com/customers/partnerstack)

Play

Uh-oh! It looks like your ad blocker is preventing the video from playing.

Please watch it on [YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_EUC1oXahDg)

![](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=%2Ffront-static%2Fillustrated-icons%2FcatLookingUp.png&w=384&q=75)

1

Building one source of truth
----------------------------

Shipping projects means delivering value.

“If you can’t execute projects well, you fail as a business,” says Tyler Calder, PartnerStack CMO. “Especially in this market. The la la land of tech has faded. So, what value are you pushing to market? If it’s not real, you’re not going to exist.”

PartnerStack automates the process of helping companies scale their ecosystem of partners, from affiliates to referrals to resellers. They’re pioneering a new model for B2B SaaS, and understand the need to move quickly when prioritizing, planning, and executing projects.

But excess tooling slowed them down at each phase in the process. Teams would start with competing tools for how to run a project, wasting the first week figuring out how to collaborate. Then, there was no source of truth for project information — forcing teams to search through siloed tools to find what they needed. And finally, there wasn’t a repeatable, scalable process all teams could follow.

So PartnerStack consolidated all its project tools and workflows into Notion. The result wasn’t just cost-saving — it was greater efficiency and thus, increased ability to deliver value.

![Map illustration](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2FveBmBp2PA30wNLAzcNqPH%2F47127591afeb2f69d4daa116d4425dee%2Fnotion_landing_spots_mgmt_map_1.png&w=1080&q=75)

2

Using Notion as a single project management tool saves time, money, and operational headaches
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

PartnerStack works in cross-functional, project-based teams. But the first 10% of a project’s timeline was wasted figuring out how those teams would work together — everyone brought different tools and mental models for how to collaborate.

Tool bloat hurt the project’s execution, too. “Things were everywhere,” says Sid Nagpal, PartnerStack Senior Manager of Customer and Partner Marketing. “Meeting notes, tasks, assigning things to one another, leaving comments, feedback.”

There wasn’t one place to see all project planning or work — creating a huge timesuck when teams wanted to get updates, find information, or track work.

PartnerStack solved this problem by consolidating all its existing project management tools into Notion. They saved $20,000 in the process, but cost wasn’t the only factor. “There’s also non-financial impacts, which over time, impact your financials,” says Tyler. “With Notion, everyone moves faster.”

All of PartnerStack’s projects are organized in Notion. Each has goals, individual tasks, and important docs like customer research and meeting notes. There’s less context-switching and searching for information, so teams at PartnerStack can focus on doing impactful work instead of project management overhead.

PartnerStack uses a Notion database for their projects to streamline organization and enhance collaboration.

![](https://www.notion.com/front-static/pages/case-studies/quote-left-rectangle.png)

For each team working on a project, we’re saving about 10 hours a week.

![Tyler Calder](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F2VX8DaTXP2MaFPKz6Geplv%2F9c4d2eea8839fe213884d4d55523fa4b%2FProject_Management_Tyler_Calder.png&w=2048&q=75)

Tyler Calder  
CMO

3

Creating scalable processes — that can be customized to projects or teams — leads to increased shipping cadence
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Most project management tools are rigid, forcing teams to shoehorn their processes into the tool’s predetermined way of working. This is especially painful when cross-functional teams come together, where each team has specific operational needs. What happens most times is teams use their own tools, information gets siloed, process is nonexistent, and shipping cadence shifts into neutral.

PartnerStack uses Notion to create a shared, scalable process everyone can follow while also being flexible enough for all teams to use. “Notion’s ability to customize and scale across functions is exactly what we needed to execute our projects,” says Tyler.

PartnerStack has been able to achieve this workflow through Notion templates. There are a set of standard templates as the starting point for projects — which are meant to be customized depending on the needs of the project or the team using them. Marketing has a specific section for data in their template, while design has callouts to include product mock-ups.

“There’s no one way to solve a problem,” says Jonathan Mendes, PartnerStack’s Co-founder and Senior Product Designer. “Templates guide you. With customization in Notion, you can solve whatever problem you want.”

Teams love Notion because it’s so flexible. Project managers and executives love it because there’s a standard, repeatable project process all teams adhere to.

PartnerStack utilizes customizable Notion templates to create scalable, team-specific processes that boost project efficiency and shipping cadence.

![](https://www.notion.com/front-static/pages/case-studies/quote-right-oval.png)

Our ability to ship individual products increased by 30%. We ship more than anybody in our industry, which wasn't always the case.

![Tyler Calder](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F2VX8DaTXP2MaFPKz6Geplv%2F9c4d2eea8839fe213884d4d55523fa4b%2FProject_Management_Tyler_Calder.png&w=2048&q=75)

Tyler Calder  
CMO

4

One source of truth for projects, uniting information from specialized tools
----------------------------------------------------------------------------

Think about all the information that’s part of a project — goals, product specs, user research, design mocks, launch plans, the kitchen sink (for good measure). Where’s it all live? Probably in a bunch of separate tools. That means there’s no source of truth for projects, which require a breadth of context for teams to operate with clarity.

“We point people to a project page, right in Notion, and they can understand what we’re doing, why we’re doing it, and why it’s important,” says Tyler.

When Jonathan and the design team were working on a new feature, they pulled everything into a single Notion page: prototypes, data, user feedback, product requirement documents, and more. This provided everyone working on the project with all the information needed to focus on solving the problem, instead of searching through other tools to get context. “Notion creates a store,” Jonathan says. “One place where everyone can come and find all documentation around the problem, notes, or explorations.”

Because Notion’s embeds and integrations bring information in from everywhere, teams don’t need to go into another team’s specialized tools. This reduces friction, making access to information easier — while reducing seat licenses to those specialized tools in the process.

PartnerStack uses Notion to seamlessly integrating Figma files and other apps to centralize project information and enhance team collaboration.

![](https://www.notion.com/front-static/pages/case-studies/quote-left-blob.png)

We've eliminated upwards of 70 seats across other platforms. We can plug any data source into Notion we need, bringing visibility to the project.

![Tyler Calder](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F2VX8DaTXP2MaFPKz6Geplv%2F9c4d2eea8839fe213884d4d55523fa4b%2FProject_Management_Tyler_Calder.png&w=2048&q=75)

Tyler Calder  
CMO

5

Project planning, tasks, and updates live in one place, helping teams work more efficiently
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

By consolidating project tools and workflows into Notion, PartnerStack was able to save time for its teams executing projects. People and processes were more organized, connected, and aligned — enabling PartnerStack to create valuable products, and deliver those products to customers with more urgency.

# How Sendbird created company-wide transparency with Notion

![Sendbird](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F2hrtmFKid2r0yUfvXrLz87%2F90065a9a7c8a24d339c634f9784b95f8%2FSendbird_Logo_RGB_lg.png&w=3840&q=75)

How Sendbird created company-wide transparency with Notion
==========================================================

Sendbird improves user engagement and retention with scalable in-app chat, voice, and video API. Notion helps them catalog knowledge, information, and ideas across the entire company.
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Insights from

![](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F3rgFYE2mhglKQNWd2cLdhc%2F1eacb2255798deb9a7b3ac3856c00f2a%2FSendbird_Eric_Kim_2.png&w=96&q=80)

JaeYeon (Eric) Kim

Product Manager

Use cases

Documentation, Wiki

1

Mission control for a fast-growing org
--------------------------------------

Sendbird has tripled its customer base since 2019 with internal headcount not far behind. They needed a way to onboard new employees and create a working environment biased toward action and results.

They built team workspaces on Notion, which every new employee sees upon joining. With information neatly arranged in subpages, it turns a potentially daunting experience into an organized portal that enables org-wide transparency. Now, even when Sendbird is hiring aggressively, new employees can hit the ground running because they have access to knowledge from day one — delivering immediate value.

![post-its](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F5pXLfatACSKeDDaYV5Oudo%2F643521f8b316602e44b8c7a6906184c6%2Fpost-its.png&w=1080&q=75)

Give your teams the perfect start on Notion.

![](https://www.notion.com/front-static/pages/case-studies/quote-left-rectangle.png)

We use our homepages in Notion to organize our thoughts.

![JaeYeon (Eric) Kim](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F3rgFYE2mhglKQNWd2cLdhc%2F1eacb2255798deb9a7b3ac3856c00f2a%2FSendbird_Eric_Kim_2.png&w=2048&q=75)

JaeYeon (Eric) Kim  
Product Manager

2

A living repository of customer knowledge
-----------------------------------------

App development is a heavy technical lift. To add Sendbird’s chat platform to existing apps, engineers learn a lot about each customer’s product — how it’s architected, how it’s maintained, who the key stakeholders are, and so on. Before, this valuable, in-depth information lived exclusively in engineers' heads, making information sharing a chore.

Sendbird built out a Notion database packed full of individual customer insights. As they’re working, engineers add insights and observations to customer entries — architecture diagrams, conversation transcripts, and technical documentation. It takes the knowledge out of their heads and into Notion where it acts as a shared collective brain.

Now, new engineers can search through tons of acquired knowledge, learning about the customer’s app and getting up to speed in minutes.

![Sendbird 2](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2FLSZJKukL8PSh5K0NbxauH%2Fa7eb9a9fed711e7366a5072e314bdb35%2Fcropped-desktop-left-1.png&w=3840&q=75)

Onboard engineers to new customer projects in minutes, not weeks.

3

As industries grow and change, Sendbird stays on trend
------------------------------------------------------

Name an industry and you can bet Sendbird has a client in it. Each vertical has its own app development eccentricities and learning the ins-and-outs of each vertical took months, if not years.

Sendbird resolved to share insights from its team to give every developer the knowledge of an industry expert, accelerating customer implementations.

Their developers came together and condensed disorganized Google Docs into a public-facing wiki on Notion. Now, everyone can access in-depth industry information on their own, reducing the burden on Sendbird’s engineers to answer simple questions, queries, and concerns.

![Bookshelf](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F5YqnDK85MAPMBq4Yt94gsm%2Fadc95daa0dabc8eb984246a38e2a6d6e%2Fbookshelf.png&w=1080&q=75)

Share institutional knowledge with the whole world.

![](https://www.notion.com/front-static/pages/case-studies/quote-right-oval.png)

Our sales reps also use Notion as a learning center. It helps them answer more technical questions from prospects instantly.

![JaeYeon (Eric) Kim](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F3rgFYE2mhglKQNWd2cLdhc%2F1eacb2255798deb9a7b3ac3856c00f2a%2FSendbird_Eric_Kim_2.png&w=2048&q=75)

JaeYeon (Eric) Kim  
Product Manager

4

An inspiration board that informs product development
-----------------------------------------------------

The messaging space moves at the speed of light. To stay at the front of the industry, Sendbird takes inspiration from everywhere — like feedback processes from SaaS companies or feature activity reports from an investment platform. Before, this inspiration lived in Pinterest boards, Google Docs, and Evernotes.

Now, when someone stumbles on a good idea, they save it to a centralized database on Notion. Every few weeks, the relevant team members vote on their favorites. The most popular ideas move onto the official product roadmap and eventually into Sendbird’s product.

Notion keeps ideas on one platform. Instead of ideas getting lost in Google Docs or Evernotes, good ideas flow from Reference Collection to Roadmap to Live with zero friction.

“Notion makes it easier for us to run through experiments,” says JaeYeon. “It’s easier for us to present ideas, vote on our favorites, and select ones to develop.”

![Sendbird 4](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F52g1rSlyLtC5qsAfOUV0ne%2Ff7381e2736f98cc5b380731b38a51579%2Fcropped-desktop-left-2.png&w=3840&q=75)

Research, planning, and execution — all on Notion.

Create a knowledge base with Notion

# Paper ships faster with crystal clear processes on Notion

![Paper](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F2IKcZNUliNJUHrd0q6fBF4%2Ff60858bb6f565feb3d738d57227c34b2%2FPaper_logo.png&w=1920&q=75)

Paper ships faster with crystal clear processes on Notion
=========================================================

Paper helps every student reach their potential with intensive one-on-one tutoring. With a clearly defined operating process, team hub, and shared working spaces on Notion, complex collaboration is effortless and enjoyable.
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Insights from

![](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F6x9Hn4cA2CCZVXvKFIZiyr%2Fa71fcc0caa119e169a877d9e832e310c%2FBrian_Blount_2.png&w=96&q=80)

Brian Blount

VP of Technology

![](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F37fVhMlTn4LQ8Kvzc4use4%2Fe3fe26f8367bbc49e96d941b6b93b72d%2FRock_Sfeir.png&w=96&q=80)

Rock Sfeir

Chief Of Staff

Use cases

Documentation, Wiki, Project Management

1

New employees contribute from day one
-------------------------------------

When Paper was just a dozen engineers, they didn’t need a written software development lifecycle cycle (SDLC) process. Everyone picked things up naturally. But as the company grew and the development team expanded, it became harder to get new engineers up to speed.

They designed their SDLC, embedded the visual in Notion, and broke each complex stage into easily digestible pieces on sub-pages.

Documenting their process on Notion officially defined how Paper works. Now, there’s no variance between teams and individuals. It reduced friction in projects, increased delivery speed, and enhanced collaboration.

![checklist illustration](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F7jm99M3VUXjuczgmwx8tni%2F11404719d24cee91df615ae9641d98f7%2Fclipboard.png&w=1080&q=75)

Turn informal ways of working into repeatable processes with Notion.

![](https://www.notion.com/front-static/pages/case-studies/quote-left-rectangle.png)

If something isn’t written down, it’s folklore.

![Brian Blount](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F6x9Hn4cA2CCZVXvKFIZiyr%2Fa71fcc0caa119e169a877d9e832e310c%2FBrian_Blount_2.png&w=2048&q=75)

Brian Blount  
VP of Technology

2

A common language boosts engineering collaboration
--------------------------------------------------

Paper’s engineers work in independent squads that focus on different parts of the product — mobile, ecosystem, service operations, and so on. Having lots of smaller teams meant they developed their own processes, assets, and jargon. Before, when squads collaborated, it felt like they were speaking different languages.

Once Paper defined its organization-wide SDLC, they began creating repeatable templates in Notion for things like project plans, problem briefs, and technical design documentation. The new artifacts formed the backbone of Paper’s new, standardized process.

With universally agreed upon definitions and templates for each artifact, Paper’s SDLC acts like a common engineering language for the startup. It creates crystal clarity on deliverables and processes that allows them to move quickly. When someone asks for a standardized problem brief, everyone knows precisely what to expect.

![Paper-2](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F3TYXTaIUyauWMIVhKWHVwG%2F736d1c29e79cf77d59a31bf1f873c7b0%2FPaper-2.png&w=3840&q=75)

Standardized templates ensure Paper’s engineers tackle problems the same way.

3

Robust processes that thrive during exponential growth
------------------------------------------------------

Since Paper supports nearly two million learners, they need a lot of tutors. As they grew, Paper's Tutor Management team faced a challenge: how do they support these tutors at scale?

They use Notion as a centralized platform for coordination and organization. Everything’s in there — standard operating procedures, job aids, onboarding guide, and so on.

By creating a repeatable process, template, or framework on Notion, they can roll it out infinitely, multiplying their impact without increasing their workload.

![tools](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2FLcw9UWJuoU29oL3483rp7%2Fc3326fb2e77de42fef57a90c3d198060%2Ftools.png&w=1080&q=75)

Onboarding one employee is as easy as one hundred

![](https://www.notion.com/front-static/pages/case-studies/quote-right-oval.png)

With Notion, missed onboarding steps for tutors have decreased from eight per cohort to just two — and we expect that to fall to zero!

![Rock Sfeir](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F37fVhMlTn4LQ8Kvzc4use4%2Fe3fe26f8367bbc49e96d941b6b93b72d%2FRock_Sfeir.png&w=2048&q=75)

Rock Sfeir  
Chief Of Staff

4

Building a long-term memory for crucial company decisions
---------------------------------------------------------

As Paper grew so too did their meeting count — executive meetings, standups, syncs, and more. Before, they used one running Google doc to manage meetings. Unfortunately, it quickly grew disorganized and unwieldy. After a few meetings, old entries got lost in the noise.

They built a Notion template based on Level 10 Meetings and began using it for all their core meetings. The template provides the meeting’s structure, then attendees add agenda items through the week and assign priorities to each talking point. During the meeting, the meeting organizer adds action items and notes.

Instead of meeting notes living in one messy document, they’re now in a searchable, filterable, and sortable database. It created a record of Paper’s meetings — a sort of long-term memory for the company. If someone wants to look at why they made a decision, it means a quick search, not hours of scrolling.

![Paper-4](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F7Dm7LgbgPLccVLK2khz1Vi%2F914e68e5169d3050714bd9444ccbba0f%2FPaper-4.png&w=3840&q=75)

A tried and tested template keeps meetings short, snappy, and action-oriented.

Make collaboration effortless on Notion

# August 13, 2024 – Notion 2.44: Introducing charts + a sneak peek at forms

### Create a chart in seconds

Turn any database into a chart in one click! Charts help you and your team track projects, goals, sales, or any info you keep in Notion. Try them for free—and if you love them, create unlimited charts on any paid plan.

![Task List New 2 (1)](https://images.ctfassets.net/spoqsaf9291f/5EtF9CjkdFcTL3pEX2HYTR/3b7735d3e22ee56c000896e91d8407d7/Task_List_New_2__1_.gif)

### Place charts on any page

It’s easy to add charts to any Notion page or database. Just type `/chart` and pick your source. When the source data changes, your chart will update automatically. No more pasting screenshots that quickly fall out of date.

### Use multiple charts to track everything

Nobody likes flipping between apps to stay on top of projects. Now you don’t have to: you can add multiple charts on a page, then click and drag them anywhere to make a custom layout. And embed analytics from other tools like Tableau and Amplitude to create your dream dashboard!

### Customize your charts

Choose from bar, line, or pie charts and change color palettes, axis names, data labels, and more. Charts should be as beautiful as they are useful!

### Smarter, more flexible automations

Automations got a big upgrade. You can now specify more than one condition to trigger an action—for example, when a task is marked “overdue” _and_ “high-priority”, automatically notify the team. Or set them to run only when a specific value is met, like when an issue is marked "at risk”. Plus, automations can do new things, like adding or removing people assigned to a project. [Learn more →](https://www.notion.so/help/database-automations)

![automation-edit](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F2BNsnA5y0QMSxEaVLVNpC1%2F5eae352c1ca5245440e3b0c6ffd1c708%2Fautomation-edit.png&w=3840&q=75)

### Next up, Forms! `Coming Soon`

You’ve been asking for this feature, and we listened. You’ll now be able to request feedback, post surveys, and collect RSVPs or whatever info you need. All responses will automatically populate your Notion database—no more logging results manually. We’ll start opening access in the coming weeks! 😄

![image (6) (1)](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2Fes9VGWLxMfUIOfqKISWWN%2F9b83f37d4602b2709dd656168be351d1%2Fimage__6___1_.png&w=3840&q=75)

### More new features

*   `Coming soon` **Chart drilldown**: Soon you'll be able to click on any data point to review its underlying pages.
    
*   **Add comments on database properties:** Ping a colleague or add a note to any database property.
    
*   **Get a sneak peek of desktop tabs:** When your cursor hovers over a tab in the desktop app, you’ll see a preview of that tab’s content.
    
*   **Add links to images:** Now you can direct any image to a specific page or URL.

# Entrepreneur Tommy Barav built a second brain in Notion

Entrepreneur Tommy Barav built a second brain in Notion
=======================================================

As the founder of Supertools, a company using AI to help people use their time more effectively, Tommy Barav made productivity his business. To no surprise, self-improvement is important in his personal life, too. With Notion, Tommy has a custom productivity and improvement system that connects and tracks all his goals in one spot.
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Insights from

![](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F1JPK6pCyOfOW5rIkvkB359%2F1ab683acca5efac0172b586add910901%2FTommy_Barav.jpeg&w=96&q=80)

Tommy Barav

Founder

Use cases

Personal Self-Improvement

1

The morning routine that sets his day up for success
----------------------------------------------------

In Tommy's opinion, self-improvement is so difficult because we're all too busy to make space for it. So he created a system that forces him to focus every morning on what's most important, and put the entire day into perspective. And that system is repeatable — giving him time back instead of forcing him to re-create his routine every day.

![coffee cups](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F4qnI6qtSTGGiaRDVfMuJEt%2F4d9d69a38a063579754753075eeda3fa%2Fcoffee-cups.png&w=1080&q=75)

Tommy uses a Notion template to set intentions for every day.

### One page to set his intentions

Every day starts with his Notion homepage, with a dedicated section for reflection. He created a template that he copies each morning, prompting him with questions that help him set his intentions for the whole day. What's he grateful for? What's one thing he learned yesterday? What's the most important thing to accomplish today? The answers to these questions serve as his North Star, helping him budget his time for the right things. He even archives previous days, so he has a record of past growth and accomplishments.

![](https://www.notion.com/front-static/pages/case-studies/quote-left-rectangle.png)

I can create a template, build a history of what I've accomplished, and capture it in one place. That's really meaningful for my self-improvement.

2

A personalized reading list, right at your digital doorstep
-----------------------------------------------------------

Reading is an essential part of Tommy's goal to always be learning, so he dedicates time for it every day. But he found news apps — using algorithms to curate his media diet — frustrating. They didn't feel personalized, and he was spending too much time scrolling for gems.

![newspaper](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F3xY3Qmujt2CwAbK62QfIB7%2Fa269c2bf6d37b4d9ab1239999c4f6f71%2Fnewspaper.png&w=1080&q=75)

Tommy uses a Notion database to curate his personal reading list.

### The news feed he's always wanted

Tommy created a Notion page just for sourcing articles he wants to read. He even shared this page with his friends and colleagues so they could also add to it. Using Notion's web clipper, articles are added to the page then categorized with tags. He's also linked this list to his Notion homepage so it serves him what he wants to see next. And once he reads them? He simply checks a box, and the next one appears on his list.

![](https://www.notion.com/front-static/pages/case-studies/quote-right-oval.png)

Instead of waiting for a tool to be updated or an app to get my dream feature, I can create my own productivity tools in Notion.

3

One place for the mind-body connection
--------------------------------------

With so much time dedicated to mental productivity, it's easy for the physical side of things to get overlooked. Tommy keeps his health goals top of mind by putting them right in his Notion homepage, instead of needing to log meals or track calories in a separate app.

![zen rocks](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F1XN3DHtPPcnhv8hBkL4sDj%2F65d25042883b2ce3d88927f4897fc2cc%2Fzen-rocks.png&w=1080&q=75)

Tommy uses a Notion table to track all his meals and make sure he’s hitting his goals.

### Keeping good habits front and center

Every day on Tommy's homepage includes a table that has empty slots for breakfast, lunch, and dinner. For each meal, he chooses from the dozens of recipes that he's saved as individual Notion pages, complete with macronutrient breakdowns for each — fat, protein, carbs, and total calories. When all meals are logged, the table automatically calculates the totals, which Tommy measures against his daily goals.

At the end of every day, he's able to look at his homepage and see exactly where he netted out on all his objectives — and what he can do better when he sets his intentions tomorrow.

![](https://www.notion.com/front-static/pages/case-studies/quote-left-blob.png)

Notion allows me to be hyper effective because all the things I want to promote are part of the same workflow and view.

Build your own self-improvement system
--------------------------------------

Notion is free to try and works across many devices.
----------------------------------------------------

# Nathan Maingard powers his creative empire with Notion

Nathan Maingard powers his creative empire with Notion
======================================================

Modern troubadour Nathan Maingard tells stories in many different ways — podcasts, films, songs, written words. With so many different mediums, there’s a lot of logistics to organize before he can start producing content. Notion cuts out the busy work so that Nate can focus on his craft.
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Insights from

![](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F7cRDKUWQOUzEHuFXAmNZ4Z%2F06a0930ad34ea470ba11ed521837f973%2FMainguard.png&w=96&q=80)

Nathan Maingard

Modern Troubadour

Use cases

Personal Organization, Personal Writing, Personal Notes

1

An artist's journal you'll never lose
-------------------------------------

Nathan’s stories start as seeds — a character, a melody, a thought. Ideas move from his notebooks into Notion. He uses Notion as a space to cultivate everything that he’s thinking about and working on. Filters help him track progress — a seed is an idea, a sapling is a project, and a fruit is a finished piece of content.

With enough care, his ideas grow and develop in unexpected directions. He can access his "Forest of Stories" on his phone or laptop and quickly discover or revisit all the ideas he's ever had.

![Flying Pages Illustration](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2FL4hXccwpynIBdXZaaBixF%2Fca9f5ee8295f003d24cf03764d2548c5%2Fflying-pages.png&w=1080&q=75)

Nathan’s Forest of Stories in Notion keeps his ideas safe and secure forever.

![](https://www.notion.com/front-static/pages/case-studies/quote-left-rectangle.png)

I know immediately what some ideas will be. Others could become a cool podcast episode, a song, or a bunch of other things. Notion helps me keep track of where ideas are going.

![Nathan Maingard](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F7cRDKUWQOUzEHuFXAmNZ4Z%2F06a0930ad34ea470ba11ed521837f973%2FMainguard.png&w=2048&q=75)

Nathan Maingard  
Modern Troubadour

2

Kickstarting songs and stories with templates
---------------------------------------------

Just getting started on a song or a story is tough. There’s a lot of logistical work before Nathan puts pen to paper. For each medium, he uses a Notion template that automatically fires up a creative workspace.

“It sounds counterintuitive, but there's many repeatable parts of the creative process,” he says. “Templates help me get clear on how I want to take a story from the beginning to the end.”

His template creates a blueprint for the story arc: Who is the character? What do they want? What are the stakes? By using Notion to automate all his prep work, Nate frees up time and energy to focus on the act of creation.

![Maingard 2](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F2EOfvRrJOlPy73gSlGZkIN%2Fa0cf4526015afd7794c25eb4154ac158%2FMaingard_2.png&w=3840&q=75)

Small prompts in templates help Nathan remember his goal for each piece of work.

3

Task planning drives progress
-----------------------------

With so many planned projects, it’s easy for Nathan to lose motivation, particularly when he’s consumed by busy work. By assigning each project a due date, Nathan creates simple and straightforward task lists.

He can view not just projects due that day and week, but also what he’s got coming up later — giving him peace of mind that he won’t forget an important deadline or milestone.

![checklist illustration](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F7jm99M3VUXjuczgmwx8tni%2F11404719d24cee91df615ae9641d98f7%2Fclipboard.png&w=1080&q=75)

Different views give Nathan insight into what’s due today, this week, and in the future.

![](https://www.notion.com/front-static/pages/case-studies/quote-right-oval.png)

My home dashboard in Notion isn't any more complicated than it needs to be. It's just a quick way for me to see what I'm working on any given day.

![Nathan Maingard](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F7cRDKUWQOUzEHuFXAmNZ4Z%2F06a0930ad34ea470ba11ed521837f973%2FMainguard.png&w=2048&q=75)

Nathan Maingard  
Modern Troubadour

4

One workspace for creation, launch, and reflection
--------------------------------------------------

Creating individual pieces of art is hard, highly creative work — but it’s only half the equation. Nathan also has to nail all the last mile work necessary to get projects out into the world.

Notion pages act as a central hub for each project, allowing Nathan to control his release process. Whether it's hosting a livestream or sharing a photo on Instagram, he’s able to relax because he knows that every step of the process is written down. For example, when he shares a story on Patreon, he ticks off the checkbox and moves onto the next task.

Nathan continues to use Notion even after a piece of art is live. His “Garden of Love” collects emails, tweets, and messages from his audience. It's a cherished collection of positive feedback that Nathan can dip into when he needs a boost.

“A lot of creatives including me struggle with impostor syndrome,” he says. “It's been epic to have all my feedback in one place so I can read and re-read them.”

![Maingard 4](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F5Z29WPhKLO9wf0eHo4cFjj%2Faf437e0f08fd10492caae9a338902cdd%2FMaingard_4.png&w=3840&q=75)

Nathan stores feedback alongside his work in Notion, closing the loop on the creative process.

One workspace for creativity and process
----------------------------------------

-----------------------------------------

Join Notion's Affiliate Program.


====================================

Refer Notion to your friends and followers, and earn up to $50 per activated sign up plus 20% of year one revenue for each referral you drive. ⚠️  Program is currently not accepting new affiliates.

How it works.
-------------

Join the program

Apply to become a Notion affiliate.

![Acme Inc company wiki](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F5t0X9gE2uE6ZbsiYw8VuQu%2Fb44e3fdbbfe4245c708ea519eb1f331f%2Flighthouse.png&w=1080&q=75)

Earn rewards

![Acme Inc company wiki](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F6HgTARmCu6bs7JCt9cQJhX%2F3eb4046d9177de44f691e1c3bdb3455a%2Fplant.png&w=1080&q=75)

> I love sharing the tools I build inside of Notion. And Notion's affiliate program lets me get paid doing the things I already wanted to do.

**Thomas Frank**Template Creator

Who can apply?
--------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Bloggers and writers | ✓   |
| Entrepreneurs | ✓   |
| Educators | ✓   |
| Media networks (news, podcasts) | ✓   |
| Email newsletters | ✓   |
| Content creators & influencers | ✓   |
| Notion community leaders, ambassadors, and template creators | ✓   |
| AI influencers & content creators | ✓   |

FAQs
----

What is the Notion Affiliate Program?

The affiliate program is a way for bloggers, writers, community owners, entrepreneurs, influencers, Notion creators, and others to spread the word about Notion and earn financial rewards.

What qualifies as a successful conversion?

Net new workspaces that upgrade to a paid Plus or Business plan within 180 days of clicking your affiliate link will be eligible for the affiliate commission. Commissions are paid on a last-click basis, so your link must be the last touch point before the user signed up.

How can I create, track, and promote my affiliate link?

You can create multiple links and see your stats in your dashboard by signing in to [PartnerStack](https://notion.partnerstack.com/)
.

Once you receive your personalized affiliate link, we recommend that you promote it on your blog, social profile, website, videos, etc (the more places the better).

When you post or distribute content about Notion, your message must make it obvious that you have a financially compensated relationship with Notion. We need all promotions to be FTC compliant. A helpful guide can be found [here](https://www.ftc.gov/business-guidance/resources/disclosures-101-social-media-influencers)
.

What if a user adds additional seats to their workspace after their first invoice?

If additional seats are being bought, we will consider it an additional transaction. As long as the customer signed up within a year, a reward will be generate for each new transaction.

What if a user downgrades?

If a user downgrades from a paying monthly plan to a free plan or receives a refund within the first 2 months of the subscription we may claw back the reward or deduct the amount from future commissions.

Can I refer myself?

Self-referrals are not allowed. The spirit of the program is to reward you for referring other people. This is not a way to get a discount on your own account.

What terms apply to the Notion Affiliate Program?

The [Notion Marketing Program Participant Terms](https://www.notion.so/Marketing-Program-Participant-Terms-c17a5869de9b4a22b91c610585b8ceca?_gl=1*1c00vsl*_gcl_au*MTEyNzU5MTU0OS4xNzM2OTU2NTY4*_ga*MTgyNjkyNzg0MS4xNzI5MTA5MzMx*_ga_9ZJ8CB186L*MTczNzAzODUyMC4xNDIuMS4xNzM3MDQwOTUxLjQ0LjAuMA..)
 apply to your participation in the Affiliate Marketing Program along with this [Program Resource Guide](https://notion.notion.site/Notion-Affiliate-Program-Resource-Center-77a6852e38d24d909ebc62960e6d90f2)
 and any Program Policies that we may make available to you from time to time.

Have more questions?

If you have more questions, you can reach out to us at [\[email protected\]](https://www.notion.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#630205050a0f0a02170610230e0208060d0c170a0c0d4d000c0e)

# Toyota, Affirm, Mercury, and more get faster business results with certified Notion Solutions Partners

![Solutions Partners](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2FTOvZ1muM0nxwjM2mvWiIx%2Ff19e25aaad2e63a71dd63ce8ce9974b2%2Fnotion-wordmark-mono-black.png&w=640&q=75)

Toyota, Affirm, Mercury, and more get faster business results with certified Notion Solutions Partners
======================================================================================================

Notion’s Solutions Partner Program connects companies with certified experts who architect scalable systems, accelerate time‑to‑value, and ensure durable adoption across teams. This story spotlights real partnerships—showing how the right guide turns Notion’s potential into measurable business outcomes.
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1

How fast could you prove ROI with an expert to architect a setup tailored for your business?
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You know that feeling when you can see exactly what your team could accomplish with the right systems in place? When the vision is crystal clear, but the path to get there feels overwhelming?

Many teams dive in solo, building page by page, teamspace by teamspace. But without someone who can step back and see the whole picture, you often end up with piecemeal systems that work in isolation but don’t scale—or worse, create new silos that defeat the original purpose.

But some of the most successful Notion customers are those that have looked beyond their own walls for support. That’s where [Notion’s Solutions Partners](https://www.notion.com/partners/solutions-partner-program)
—certified experts who specialize in transforming vision into reality—come in. They’ve guided companies like Toyota as they rethink workflows, helped fintechs like Affirm replace entire tool stacks, and architected enterprise systems that actually stick.

![Scaffolding](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F3ctCqydbrMOfU0qMPaeyby%2Fafd9111d03078716843199f485b54972%2FScaffolding.png&w=1080&q=75)

2

Notion State enables Affirm to rapidly reach 95% adoption
---------------------------------------------------------

At Affirm, thousands of employees were navigating a patchwork of tools—Asana, Google Docs, Excel, Confluence, Coda, Slack, and more. Each team had its own workflows, which created tons of information silos that made it impossible to build a truly company-wide operating system.

Working side-by-side with leaders across IT, Legal, Marketing, Product Design, and more, Notion State delivered a comprehensive deployment strategy that balanced immediate needs with long-term scalability. Their five-phase approach, from discovery to evolution, helped Affirm build connected systems designed for scale and impact.

The results speak for themselves: 95% adoption for knowledge management, significant cost savings from eliminating or reducing seats in other tools, and a unified workspace where information is trusted, accessible, and actionable.

**[Meet Notion State →](https://www.notion.so/consulting-partners/notion-state)
**

![](https://www.notion.com/front-static/pages/case-studies/quote-left-rectangle.png)

Effective knowledge management and collaboration is essential. Working with our Solutions Partner helped us more effectively streamline tooling, eliminate redundancies, and enable every team to move faster.

![Nora Grasham](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F3m02WACW4rvDe2fHapLi5d%2F3ac91e0a379415c92f36cb4aa1dac5e7%2FNora_Grasham.png&w=1080&q=75)

Nora Grasham  
Affirm, Sr Director of IT Engineering

3

NorthSand and Toyota built automations that 3× workflow speed
-------------------------------------------------------------

Toyota’s Future Creation Center was bogged down in bottlenecks. With work scattered across multiple tools, teams spent more time searching for knowledge and prepping for meetings than innovating. What started as one employee’s effort to streamline these challenges became a complete wall-to-wall reimagining of how how their entire org works.

Working with NorthSand’s technical expertise, the team focused on custom automations, streamlined approval workflows, and centralized scheduling systems. NorthSand provided ongoing support to ensure the grassroots initiative took hold and evolved into a more robust system that could scale and support Toyota’s larger innovation goals.

With Notion, key workflows now run 3× faster, meeting prep time was cut in half, and the entire division stays aligned. Most importantly, Notion became the trusted daily tool that made cross-functional collaboration happen more naturally and peace of mind that made the work feel lighter.

**[Meet NorthSand →](https://www.notion.so/consulting-partners/northsand-inc)
**

4

MegazoneCloud helps JOYCITY unite hundreds of employees into a single, company-wide tool
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

As leading South Korean game developer JOYCITY started the transition to Notion from Jira, Confluence, and Microsoft, the process felt a bit overwhelming. They knew they needed a unified system where teams across planning, development, and business operations could collaborate more effectively. But onboarding hundreds of employees without disrupting work was daunting.

Working with MegazoneCloud, JOYCITY deployed a transition strategy that focused new projects in Notion while maintaining parallel systems during migration. MegazoneCloud’s solution engineers provided on-site training and managed technical complexities so JOYCITY could focus on work.

The shift stuck: improved collaboration, improved search and document accessibility, and a scalable foundation for future growth of the company. As teams spent more time working in Notion with MegazoneCloud’s support, teams were able to not only streamline their work but fundamentally rethink _how_ they work.

**[Meet MegazoneCloud →](https://www.megazone.com/)
**

5

Optemization transforms Mercury’s “lawless” workspace into a trusted operating system
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Mercury’s years of rapid growth created what the team called a “lawless” Notion workspace. With well over 1,000 employees across nearly ninety teamspaces, Mercury started using Notion organically but 300,000 pages of content had quickly become outdated and untrustworthy.

They turned to Optemization for for a complete workspace overhaul. After a comprehensive audit of all content, Optemization implemented a three-phase approach that included new standard templates, a unified information architecture, and an automated Notion training system for new employees.

The cleanup was massive: after archiving over 63,000 pages, Mercury now has a single source of truth that enables teams to move faster with trusted information. Consistent teamspace structure across twenty departments has made Notion even more intuitive to use company-wide.

**[Meet Optemization →](https://www.notion.so/consulting-partners/optemization)
**

6

The best outcomes happen when brilliant minds work together
-----------------------------------------------------------

We’ve always believed our community is our greatest strength. The creativity, innovation, and problem solving that happens when people come together around shared challenges is what makes the Notion community special.

Our Solutions Partners represent the best of that community—experts who have dedicated themselves to mastering Notion and helping others be successful. We want to ensure the companies that need these skills most can find the right partner to work with easily.

**Ready to find yours? [Head to our Solutions Partner directory →](https://www.notion.so/consulting-partners)
**

# July 14, 2021 – Better mobile apps & public pages 🌐

### Brand new formatting toolbar on iOS & Android

Your Notion mobile app just got a big upgrade! The formatting toolbar above your keyboard is completely redesigned from the ground up.

![Mobile editing](https://www.notion.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fspoqsaf9291f%2F30nBqkDEN9iiwIzxeGkvN7%2Fa80b5dc8a13f2c96a11b5db52d1f23c8%2FWriting_and_Editing_Reference_Visuals__1_.png&w=3840&q=75)

Now it's much easier to add & edit content on mobile — the toolbar intelligently recommends formatting options depending on the content you're working with. Changing the text & highlight color, notifying your teammates in a comment, indenting bullet points... it all feels faster and more natural in the new formatting toolbar.

[Download the mobile app →](https://www.notion.com/mobile)

### Public pages are now hosted at \[yourdomain\].notion.site

You've always been able to turn any Notion page into a quick & easy website in just a couple clicks. Now the links you share with colleagues, customers and friends will include your personal branding right at the front!

*   Before, links to your public pages looked like this: notion.so/**acmedesign**/xxxxx12345
    
*   Now, links to your public pages will look like this: **acmedesign**.notion.site/xxxxx12345
    

To set the domain for your public links, just go to Settings & Members → Settings → Domain (you'll need to be on desktop, and an admin in your workspace).

[Learn more about publishing pages at yourdomain.notion.site →](https://www.notion.com/blog/personalize-public-pages)

### Better numbered lists

Now all the numbered lists across your Notion workspace will default to alternating between numbers, letters, and Roman numerals at each level of indentation

### Better quote blocks

To help maintain visual consistency across your pages, quote blocks now default to the same size as regular text. The large quote size is still available too. Just select your preference by clicking the ⋮⋮ block handle to the left of a quote block, and navigate to the new **Quote size** menu. Then all your future quotes will use that size.

### New API integration with Back

We're excited to welcome a new partner to the API platform, Back. They help companies provide a seamless employee experience by making support and information readily available.

With this integration, teams like HR and IT always have the right Notion page on hand when responding to employee requests. Back’s bots for Slack, MS Teams, and Google Chat can also use your policies and how-tos to automatically answer FAQs, such as “What’s our expense policy?” or “How do I request time off?”

[Add the Back integration to your workspace →](https://backhq.com/integrations/notion)

### Bug Fixes & improvements

*   **We now have hand-picked sans and serif typefaces for Chinese (Simplified & Traditional), Japanese & Korean in the ••• menu at the top-right corner of your pages**
    
*   **Syntax highlighting for Julia is now supported in code blocks**
    
*   Fixed a bug that prevented Korean dates from being added as date @mentions
    
*   Fixed a bug that prevented Notion from navigating to the most recently visited page when switching between workspaces
    
*   Fixed a bug that caused the **`Count unique values`** button to show incorrect results for relation properties

# May 19, 2020 – Notion 2.8, now free for personal use 🤘

### New features

*   **Add unlimited content for free 💸**  
    Previously, personal users had to pay $5/month to add as much content to Notion as they wanted. But hitting a storage limit is the last thing you should have to worry about right now. So we got rid of it.  
    Now you can use Notion on your own for free, indefinitely.  
    [See FAQs for more on this →](https://www.notion.so/What-s-New-157765353f2c4705bd45474e5ba8b46c)
    
    See how Notion can help you think, write, and get organized at [notion.com/personal →](http://www.notion.com/personal)
    
*   **Introducing the Personal Pro Plan 🧰**  
    Designed for power users who love working with others, or who need more upload power, this new plan includes all the features of the free Personal Plan, plus:
    
    *   No limit on individual file uploads (5MB is the limit for free)
        
    *   Unlimited guest collaborators on any page (the limit is 5 on the free plan)
        
    *   Version history up to 30 days
        
    *   API access (coming soon)  
        This plan costs $5 per month paid monthly, or $4 per month paid annually. If you were paying for our Personal Plan before now, you've been automatically upgraded to Pro!
        
    
*   **Enable edits on public pages 🔗**  
    Before, only guests or members in your workspace could edit a page with you. Now you can give editing access to anyone by sending them the link. People will need a Notion account to make edits, but we simplified the sign-up process for anyone coming in via your link.  
    Use this for crowdsourced projects, classroom collaboration, or simply sharing a grocery list with a loved one. [Here's a step-by-step guide →](https://www.notion.so/Public-pages-web-publishing-9a1cbda1c1b5438390b93a7ee0f481ae)
    
*   **Easy invite links for teams** 🎟  
    For Team and Enterprise users, you can invite more teammates to join your workspace with a secret link (before, you had to enter each email address manually). Just go to the `Members` section of `Settings & Members` in your sidebar: [Learn more about adding teammates with this guide →](https://www.notion.so/Add-members-to-your-workspace-27d4016f95b5444eb7be5f81d5bfa057)
    
*   **Redesigned** `**Share**` **menu 🌏**  
    The `Share` menu has a new look! We refreshed the design and the wording to make it a bit easier to share your pages however you like: with specific people, groups of people, your whole team, or with everyone on the web. [Learn how sharing works here →](https://www.notion.so/Share-your-work-49f701565a0b443c9dca27fd0aa0f074)
    
*   **New templates for writing, planning, and organizing 🧭**  
    Templates are a great way to see what's possible in Notion, or to quickly add some structure to your page. Just click `Templates` in your left-hand sidebar, add to your workspace, and customize to your needs.  
    In honor of our new free Personal Plan, we created a bunch of new templates for life management. Find them in the `Templates` window under `Personal`. [Learn how to get the most out of templates with this 2-minute video →](https://youtu.be/2Pwzff-uffU)
    

### Bug Fixes & improvements

*   The mobile apps now start 2x faster! [More on that here →](https://www.notion.so/What-s-New-157765353f2c4705bd45474e5ba8b46c)
    
*   Updated the Getting Started default pages to include more useful information after account creation
    
*   The mobile apps for iPad and Android tablets now include `←` and `→` buttons at the top left when in landscape orientation, for easier navigation
    
*   Fixed an issue that prevented users from navigating backwards through account creation steps
    
*   Fixed an issue that prevented some users from adding guests to a workspace
    
*   Fixed a bug that prevented Greek characters from rendering on Windows
    
*   Fixed a bug that prevented file blocks from migrating correctly during `Move to` → database
